issues.description: The docstring claims 'each figure sits in a tile inside a labelled role=group so assistive tech reads the value and its label together', but the implementation wraps ALL tiles in a single section[role=group] (line 178). Individual tiles are plain <div part="tile"> with no per-tile grouping, so the value/label pairing (e.g. '1.3k' + 'Views') is conveyed only by DOM order and visual proximity, not semantically. Wrap each tile in its own role=group/aria-label or use an aria-label combining value+label per tile.

The abbreviated count (formatCount converts 1280 -> '1.3k', line 11-19) is exposed to assistive tech verbatim, so screen readers announce a lossy '1.3k' instead of the exact 1,280. Provide the full value via an aria-label on the tile-value span while showing the abbreviation visually.

The section already has aria-label=this.label (line 178) and then renders

this.label

(line 179) with the identical text. Screen readers can announce the label twice (once as the group name, once as the paragraph). Consider aria-hidden on the visible title or drop the redundant group aria-label. Also the title is styled as a heading (uppercase, 700 weight) but uses a non-heading

, offering no heading semantics for navigation.

No custom events are dispatched at all. For a data-injected pattern, an event such as 'stats-rendered' (or reflecting empty vs populated state) would let consumers react to async data arriving. The component is purely write-only via the stats property/attribute.

Spacing, radius and rhythm use arbitrary off-grid rem values (padding 0.9rem, border-radius 0.95rem and 0.7rem, gap 0.6rem, title margin 0.7rem, tile gap 0.15rem) rather than a consistent 4/8px Blueprint scale, and colors are massaged with ad-hoc color-mix hacks (stroke at 82%/48% transparent, surface-secondary mixed with 'white 6%', lines 121-148) instead of consuming the design tokens directly. No font-family token is applied (relies on font: inherit).

An empty state ('No access data', line 180) is handled, but there is no loading state. Because stats arrive asynchronously via the property (the docs example injects them post-mount, examples.ts line 627), an un-set element renders the empty 'No access data' message rather than a neutral/loading placeholder, which can flash misleading content before data lands.
formatCount only abbreviates thousands: values in the millions render as e.g. '1000k' (1000000/1000 = 1000, line 15-18) instead of '1M'. Add an M (and higher) tier or cap behavior for large counts.

summary: A clean, correct presentational pattern that consumes --boe-token-* variables (with sensible fallbacks) and handles an empty state, but it is off-Blueprint on spacing/radius, dispatches no events, and its accessibility falls short of its own docstring: a single group wraps all tiles, the visible title duplicates the group label, and abbreviated counts are exposed lossily to assistive tech.

A single-select "accordion" that consumes brand/surface tokens and looks polished in light mode, but has meaningful accessibility and correctness gaps: a full innerHTML re-render on every click destroys focus, aria-controls dangles to non-existent panels, and it can never fully collapse. State coverage and dark-mode robustness are weak.

issues.description: render() replaces the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every click. When the user activates a trigger (Enter/Space/click), all buttons are destroyed and recreated, so keyboard focus is lost to <body> instead of remaining on the activated header. No focus is restored after re-render (lines 124-222).

Panels are only rendered into the DOM when open (isOpen ternary, lines 109-118), yet every trigger sets aria-controls="panel-<value>" (line 104). For all collapsed items the aria-controls reference points to an element that does not exist, breaking the WAI-ARIA accordion relationship. Panels should stay in the DOM with the `hidden` attribute.

The open item cannot be collapsed: the click handler returns early when nextValue === valueInternal (line 208), and render() force-selects items[0] when value is empty (line 83). There is always exactly one open panel and no collapsible/multiple-open mode, so this behaves as single-select radio-list, not a real accordion, and offers no 'all closed' state.

Elevation and highlights are hardcoded light-theme values baked into the markup: box-shadow uses rgba(255,255,255,0.82) insets and rgba(15,23,42,0.04), and item/indicator backgrounds mix tokens with literal `white`. A dark theme exists (box-dark.ts) but these white inset highlights and light gradients do not re-theme, so the component looks wrong in dark mode despite the token fallbacks.

The header label is a plain <span part="heading"> inside the button (line 106) with no heading element or role=heading. The WAI-ARIA accordion pattern requires each trigger to be contained in an element with a heading role so screen-reader users can navigate by heading; here the accordion exposes no heading structure at all.

Only :focus-visible is styled (lines 194-198). There is no hover state on the trigger, no active/pressed state, no disabled support, and no distinct selected/open styling — the open item is visually identical to closed items apart from the +/- glyph. Available tokens SurfaceSurfaceHover and SurfaceItemSurfaceSelected are never used, and the data-open attribute (line 97) is set but referenced by no CSS rule.

aria-label is placed on <div part="accordion"> (line 200), a generic div with no role. aria-label on a role-less generic container is ignored by most assistive tech, so the accordion group has no accessible name and is not exposed as a group/list.

The value setter (lines 59-63) calls render() directly AND setAttribute('value', ...), which triggers attributeChangedCallback -> render() again, causing a redundant double render on every programmatic value set.

Radii (1rem, 0.7rem) and spacing (0.75rem, 0.95rem, 1rem) are hardcoded literals rather than drawn from a radius/spacing token scale, so the component does not follow a shared Blueprint rhythm and cannot be retuned via tokens.

No keyboard enhancements beyond native button activation: the recommended (optional) accordion keyboard support — Up/Down arrow to move between headers, Home/End to jump to first/last — is not implemented.
The value-changed event is only dispatched on user click (lines 214-221); programmatic value changes via the setter fire no event, and there is no toggle/collapse event, limiting host integration.

summary: A functional but under-polished alert with correct token references undermined by a genuine always-true open bug, a native `title` attribute collision, and color-only tone differentiation with a fixed politeness level. Solid token plumbing, weak states and accessibility.

issues.description: connectedCallback line 76 sets `this.openValue = this.hasAttribute("open") || !this.hasAttribute("open")`, which is a tautology that always evaluates to true. It is impossible to render an initially-dismissed alert from markup (e.g. `<box-alert>` with open omitted), and this directly contradicts attributeChangedCallback which sets openValue = hasAttribute("open"). Should be `this.openValue = !this.hasAttribute("open") ? true : this.hasAttribute("open")` per intended default, or simply reflect the attribute with an explicit default.

The component observes and overrides the native global `title` attribute/property (observedAttributes includes "title", plus a title getter/setter). Setting `title` on any HTMLElement produces a native browser tooltip on hover, so the alert heading text will also appear as an OS tooltip over the whole element — an unintended side effect. Use a non-reserved name like `heading` or `alert-title`.

The live region is hardcoded to role="status" + aria-live="polite" (line 203) regardless of tone. Error-tone alerts should use role="alert" / aria-live="assertive" so failures are announced immediately; as written an error is announced with the same low priority as an info message.

Tone is communicated by border/background color only (info/success/error/warning) with no leading status icon or text label, failing WCAG 1.4.1 (use of color). Box Blueprint alerts carry a tone icon; there is no non-color affordance distinguishing success from error here.

The alert container sets aria-label to its own visible title/message text (line 203). Labelling a live region with the same content it already contains causes redundant/double announcement by screen readers; the aria-label should be removed (the region's text children are announced automatically) or replaced with aria-labelledby referencing the title node.

State coverage is thin: the alert surface defines a background/border transition but has no hover or active state, the dismiss button implements hover and focus-visible but no :active/pressed state, and there is no loading state despite an `inprogress` tone existing. No brand focus-ring token is used — the focus outline is an ad-hoc color-mix of surface-brand at 34%.

On dismiss the shadow root innerHTML is cleared (render() sets it to ""), removing the focused Dismiss button without moving focus anywhere, so keyboard focus is silently dropped to document.body. Focus should be relocated to a sensible sibling or the host.

Spacing and radius are hardcoded rem literals rather than Blueprint spacing/radius tokens (padding 0.85rem 0.95rem, border-radius 0.95rem, content gap 0.25rem). The 0.95rem corner radius is non-standard for Box (Blueprint uses ~4-8px), and the dismiss control is a text "Dismiss" pill rather than the conventional Box icon close (X) button, reducing fidelity.

summary: A reasonably token-aware read-only panel with sensible JSON attributes and events, but it ships almost no interaction states, has no dark-mode story (hardcoded white backgrounds), weak accessibility semantics, and a native-property clash on `title`.

issues.description: The interactive elements — [part="action"] and [part="reply"] buttons — define only cursor:pointer. There are no :hover, :focus-visible (brand focus ring), or :active rules anywhere in the stylesheet. Keyboard users get no visible focus treatment and pointer users get no affordance feedback.

Dark mode is broken by hardcoded light values: [part="annotation"] uses background: rgba(255,255,255,0.72) and [part="action"][data-tone="primary"] uses color:#fff, while [part="avatar"]/badges mix brand into `white`. None respond to box-dark tokens, so the panel renders white-on-white content in a dark theme instead of using --boe-token-surface-* tokens.

No disabled state is supported or styled. AnnotationInspectorAction has no `disabled` field and buttons are always clickable/emit events, unlike the sibling annotation-toolbar which supports `disabled: true`. There is also no loading/error state.

The panel title is rendered as <div part="title"> with no heading role and the wrapping <article> has no aria-labelledby / aria-label, so assistive tech gets an unlabeled region rather than a named, navigable inspector heading.

The component overrides the native HTMLElement.title property with a custom getter/setter (returning "Annotation Inspector" default), shadowing the browser's tooltip semantics. Any consumer setting `title` for a tooltip will instead repaint the panel header, and the default forces a visible attribute. A non-conflicting name (e.g. `heading`/`label`) should be used.

The color swatch [part="color-chip"] conveys annotation color purely visually with no text alternative or aria-label, and the meta pills (tool/status/page) are unlabeled spans, so status/color information is lost to screen readers.

annotation.color is interpolated into an inline style attribute: style="--annotation-color:${escapeHtml(annotation.color)};". escapeHtml only escapes HTML entities, not CSS, so a crafted color value (e.g. containing ';') can inject additional CSS custom-property declarations into the host style attribute. The value should be validated/sanitized as a color rather than HTML-escaped.

render() replaces the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback and re-binds listeners each time. Any focus on an action/reply button is lost on re-render, and there is no reconciliation — brittle if the element is updated while a user is interacting.

Hardcoded token fallbacks drift from the real defaults: text fallbacks use #1f1e1b and #6f6f6f whereas box-defaults defines TextText as #222222; the primary action uses literal #fff instead of --boe-token-text-text-on-brand. Spacing uses off-grid values (0.42rem, 0.85rem, 0.95rem, 0.14rem) and mixed radii (1rem/0.9rem) rather than a consistent Blueprint 4/8px rhythm.

Dispatched event names are generic and unnamespaced ("action", "reply-selected"), risking collisions with other components in a bubbling/composed tree; a namespaced name (e.g. "annotation-action") would be safer for a design-system pattern.

Each entry is a native <button> whose role is overridden to role="listitem" (line 161-166). This destroys the implicit button semantics — assistive tech announces it as a list item, not an actionable control — while the wrapping container is role="list". The selection state is expressed via aria-pressed="true" (line 166), but aria-pressed is only valid on button/switch/toggle roles; on a listitem it is invalid and ignored, so the selected state is never announced to screen readers.

Selecting an entry causes focus loss. emitEntrySelected() sets this.selectedEntryId (line 122), which fires attributeChangedCallback -> render() and replaces the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML (line 186). The clicked <button> node is destroyed and recreated, so keyboard focus is dropped to the body after every selection. render() rebuilds the whole tree on any attribute change rather than patching the aria-pressed/selected state.

The public 'title' attribute/property (observedAttributes line 29, getter/setter lines 79-85) collides with the global HTML title attribute. Because it is reflected to the host's title attribute, the browser renders a native tooltip (e.g. 'Discussion') on hover over the whole component, and the getter shadows HTMLElement.prototype.title. A design-system heading should use a dedicated attribute like 'heading' or 'thread-title' to avoid the native-tooltip side effect.

No disabled or active/pressed states are implemented for either entries or action buttons, and action buttons have no :hover style at all (only [part="entry"]:hover exists, lines 244-248). Actions render a static appearance with no interactive feedback besides focus-visible.

The action tone system only styles data-tone="primary" (lines 330-334); every other tone (e.g. a destructive/'danger' action, which the AnnotationThreadAction.tone type openly allows) falls back to the neutral outline style. There is no destructive/error tone despite the data model supporting arbitrary tones.

Spacing values are arbitrary and not on the Blueprint 4px rhythm: 0.42rem, 0.72rem, 0.6rem, 0.24rem, 0.35rem, 0.18rem, 0.78rem, 1.15rem (lines 205-321). Blueprint spacing increments in 0.25rem/4px steps, so these off-scale values break vertical rhythm and consistency with sibling components.

Hardcoded non-token values: the entry hover box-shadow uses cool-slate rgba(15,23,42,0.05) (line 247) rather than a neutral token-derived shadow, and the primary action text color is hardcoded #fff (line 333) instead of the available --boe-token-text-text-on-brand token (TextTextOnBrand = #ffffff).

Card and entry radius is 1rem/16px (lines 200, 233) which is heavier than Blueprint's typical 4-8px component radii; combined with 999px pills the component reads softer/rounder than the rest of the Box system.

Selection/content changes are not surfaced to assistive tech — there is no aria-live region and, more importantly, no roving-tabindex or arrow-key navigation for the list of entries; users must Tab through each button individually with no list-navigation affordance expected of a thread list.

summary: A reasonably styled thread pattern that correctly uses --boe-token-* variables and escapes all interpolated HTML, but it ships real semantic bugs (button role overridden to listitem plus aria-pressed invalidated, so selection is never announced) and loses focus on every selection due to full innerHTML re-render. Missing disabled/active/hover states, off-scale Blueprint spacing, and a title-attribute collision keep it below production quality.

issues.description: No focus-visible styling anywhere. Tool/color/action buttons never render a brand focus ring, so keyboard users get only the UA default (often suppressed by appearance:none on many engines). The design system exposes surface-brand (#0061d5) but it is never used for a focus outline/box-shadow. This is a fundamental keyboard-a11y gap for an interactive toolbar.

Both the tools and colors containers declare role="toolbar" (lines 346, 352) but the ARIA toolbar pattern (roving tabindex + Arrow/Home/End key navigation) is not implemented. Every button is independently Tab-focusable and there is no keydown handler, so the announced role contradicts the actual interaction model. Either implement roving focus or drop role="toolbar".

Selecting a tool or color loses focus. emitToolSelected/emitColorSelected set an attribute (active-tool-id / current-color) which fires attributeChangedCallback -> render(), and render() replaces shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale (line 220). The clicked button is destroyed and recreated, so focus falls back to document.body. Keyboard users are ejected from the toolbar on every activation.

No hover or active/pressed states on any control. tools, colors, and actions have no :hover or :active rules, despite the token set providing --boe-token-surface-surface-hover, --boe-token-surface-surface-brand-hover, and --boe-token-surface-surface-brand-pressed. The controls feel static and give no affordance feedback.

Actions cannot be disabled and only the "primary" tone is styled. AnnotationToolbarAction has no disabled field and the rendered <button> never emits a disabled attribute (lines 203-218). data-tone defaults to "neutral" but only [data-tone="primary"] has styling — a destructive action like Redact/Delete cannot be visually distinguished (no danger/warning tone), even though --boe-token-surface-status-surface-error exists.

Text-on-brand color is hardcoded as #fff for the active tool (line 293) and primary action (line 334) instead of the available --boe-token-text-text-on-brand token. The selected-color ring also uses a hardcoded cool-slate shadow rgba(15,23,42,0.12) (line 319) rather than a neutral Box token, breaking the token contract and the warm-neutral palette.

Spacing and radius are off the Blueprint 4px grid and untokenized: gaps/padding use arbitrary values (0.9rem=14.4px, 0.85rem, 0.72rem, 0.42rem) and radii are ad-hoc (1rem container, 999px pills). Blueprint rhythm expects 4/8px multiples and defined radius tokens. Wrapping tokens in color-mix() with arbitrary percentages (82%, 94%, 60%) further dilutes token fidelity.

Mutually-exclusive tools are modeled as independent aria-pressed toggle buttons rather than a radio group, so assistive tech does not convey that exactly one tool is active. Also the tool-icon emoji span (line 173) is not aria-hidden, so decorative emoji glyphs can be announced alongside the label.

Selected-color detection compares by color.value (line 184); if two swatches share the same value both render aria-pressed="true". Selection/active state is keyed on value/id with no de-duplication or validation of empty/duplicate ids.

summary: A clean, XSS-safe pattern with a solid attribute/reflection/event API, but it falls short of Box production quality: no focus-visible or hover/active states, a claimed but unimplemented role="toolbar" keyboard pattern, and a focus-loss bug where every selection re-renders the whole shadow DOM. Token usage is mostly present but undermined by hardcoded #fff/slate values and off-grid spacing.

A minimal, purely presentational three-column layout shell that correctly consumes --boe-token-* variables with fallbacks and escapes its title, but is under-built for an "app shell": no responsiveness, hardcoded landmark labels, empty always-rendered landmarks, and a fragile override of the native title property.

issues.description: The component defines get/set title(), which overrides the native HTMLElement.title tooltip property. Consumers setting el.title (or the browser rendering a title tooltip) collide with this custom behavior; the attribute is also a global HTML attribute. A dedicated attribute like 'heading' or 'shell-title' would avoid clobbering native semantics.

The nav, aside, and footer landmark slots are always rendered even when no content is slotted into them. Empty <nav aria-label="Primary"> and <aside aria-label="Context"> regions are announced by screen readers as empty navigation/complementary landmarks. Conditionally render these regions based on slotted content (e.g. via slotchange) or hide empties.

Landmark labels are hardcoded English strings ('Primary', 'Context') and cannot be localized or customized. With multiple app-shell instances on a page these produce duplicate, indistinguishable landmark labels. Expose attributes (e.g. nav-label / aside-label) to control them.

Spacing and radius are hardcoded magic numbers (padding 0.95rem/1.15rem, gap 0.85rem, border-radius 0.95rem, nav min-width 11rem) rather than Blueprint spacing/radius scale tokens. Values like 0.95rem are off any standard 4/8px rhythm. No --boe spacing or radius tokens are consumed even though color tokens are.

observedAttributes is only ['title']. For an app shell there is no API for the features the pattern implies: collapsible/hideable nav or aside, a 'collapsed' or 'dense' state, or controlling which regions show. No CustomEvents are dispatched (e.g. nav toggle). The component's surface area is far below a production app-shell.

No responsive behavior: the frame is a fixed grid-template-columns: minmax(11rem,auto) 1fr minmax(11rem,auto) with no @media queries or container queries, so the three-column layout does not collapse on narrow viewports/mobile and will overflow. An app shell must reflow its side regions.
The heading is hardcoded to <h2>. Depending on where the shell is placed this may not match the document heading outline (a top-level app shell typically wants h1). The level should be configurable.

render() rebuilds the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback (e.g. each title change), re-parsing all styles and re-creating slots rather than updating only the title text node. Wasteful and destroys any transient shadow-DOM state on trivial attribute updates.

No brand accent or focus styling anywhere; the shell provides no focus-visible ring convention or hover/selected affordances for its (typically interactive) nav region, unlike sibling components (e.g. rating uses --boe-token-surface-surface-brand focus outlines). The Box blue accent token is never referenced.

render() writes shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback, so changing ANY attribute (e.g. size or tone) destroys and recreates the element. This forces the browser to re-fetch the image and produces a visible flash/reload on every attribute update. State should be updated in place (dedicated

The wrapper div uses aria-label but has no role="img". aria-label on a generic (non-semantic) div is not reliably exposed to assistive tech, so the avatar may be announced as raw text or not at all. The container should carry role="img" (with the fallback initials span marked aria-hidden), and when an with its own alt is rendered the container aria-label duplicates the label, producing double announcement.

There is no image error/loading handling. If the src fails to load, the is rendered with no onerror fallback to initials, leaving a broken/empty circle. A production avatar should fall back to the initials/placeholder when the image errors and ideally handle the loading state.

Invalid size produces NaN dimensions: size getter does Number(getAttribute('size')), and render() computes Math.max(24, this.size). If size is non-numeric (e.g. size="large"), this.size is NaN, Math.max(24, NaN) is NaN, and the inline style becomes width:NaNpx;height:NaNpx;, collapsing the element. Needs a NaN guard/clamp.

Fallback initials do not scale with the avatar size. The [part="fallback"] span inherits a fixed font from :host (font: inherit) regardless of the size attribute, so large avatars show tiny centered initials and very small avatars can overflow/clip. Font-size should be derived from the size (e.g. size * ~0.4).

Hardcoded colors bypass the token system. The success tone gradient hardcodes #16a34a (a warm green that is not from the Box cool-neutral/blue palette and has no --boe-token-* variable), and the box-shadow uses hardcoded rgba(15,23,42,0.05) / rgba(255,255,255,0.8). Tone surfaces and elevation shadows should reference --boe-token-* variables.

Empty/absent labels degrade poorly. When name, alt and initials are all unset, initials become '?' and the container aria-label is set to literally '?', so a screen reader announces 'question mark'. With no name the img alt also resolves to empty string, mixing a decorative image with a labelled container. There is no meaningful default accessible name.

No events are dispatched (e.g. an image load/error event), and tone accepts arbitrary strings with only 'informative'/'success'/'neutral' styled — an unknown tone silently renders the neutral background with no validation or documented enumeration. Also src is not validated/sanitized for scheme (javascript:/data: URLs are passed straight through, escaped only for attribute breakout).

summary: A presentational avatar with decent token usage and attribute reflection, but undermined by real bugs: full-subtree innerHTML rebuilds that reload the image on any attribute change, a NaN size crash, missing role=\"img\"/redundant labeling, no broken-image fallback, and initials that don't scale with size. Not yet Box production quality.

A clean, XSS-safe presentational badge that correctly consumes --boe-token-* variables, but it hardcodes literal white into every color-mix (breaking dark theme), misuses role="status" as a live region, and skips Blueprint spacing/radius tokens.

issues.description: Every tone background is computed with color-mix against the literal keyword `white` (e.g. `var(--boe-token-surface-surface-brand,#0061d5) 10%, white 90%` on lines 63, 79, 84, 90). Because white is hardcoded rather than a surface token, badges stay pale/light even when box-dark is active, so the component does not re-theme in dark mode despite pulling brand/status colors from tokens. Mix against --boe-token-surface-surface (or -surface-secondary) instead.

Line 94 applies `role="status"` to every badge. role=status is an aria-live=polite region, so a static label badge causes screen readers to announce (and re-announce on every re-render) its text as a live update. A badge is not a live region; drop the role, or reserve status/live semantics for genuinely dynamic counters.

Line 94 sets `aria-label` to the same escaped string that is already the span's visible text content. The redundant aria-label duplicates the accessible name for no benefit and can cause doubled/overriding announcements; remove it and let the text content provide the name. Tone (error/success) is also conveyed by color alone and never exposed to assistive tech.

Spacing, radius and type are hardcoded magic numbers rather than Blueprint tokens: padding `0.22rem 0.55rem` (line 61), `border-radius: 999px` (line 62), `font-size: 0.74rem` and `letter-spacing: 0.05em` (lines 66-67). None reference spacing/radius/typography tokens, so the badge drifts off the design-system rhythm and cannot be re-scaled by theme.

Line 70 declares `transition: background 140ms ease, color 140ms ease` but no selector ever changes this element's background/color in response to a state (badge is non-interactive with no hover/active/selected rules). The transition is effectively dead unless a token value animates, indicating copied boilerplate rather than intentional state handling.

`tone` (lines 32-38) is an unvalidated free-form string. Any unrecognized value (or the default-getter value not matching a data-tone rule) silently falls through to the brand-blue base style at lines 57-71 rather than a documented default, and there is no enumerated tone contract or reflected invalid-value handling.

render() (lines 40-96) rewrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML — recreating the full <style> block — on every attributeChangedCallback, rather than templating once and updating only the data-tone attribute and text node. Works, but is wasteful and discards any focus/selection on each attribute change.

Each bar is a <button> with role="listitem" (line 189-196). Overriding the button's implicit role with listitem removes the button/interactive semantics from assistive tech — screen readers announce these as plain list items, so the fact that they are clickable and emit point-selected is not conveyed. Use a role="list"/role="listitem" wrapper structure that keeps a real <button> inside, or drop the role override entirely.

The component reflects into the native global `title` attribute (get/set title, observedAttributes includes "title", lines 32/103-109/472). Setting `title` on the host renders a native browser tooltip over the entire element on hover and collides with the standard HTML title semantics. Rename to a non-reserved attribute like `heading`/`chart-title`.

Accent and success tones hardcode colors instead of tokens: #5a7cf7/#3f62ea for accent and #26c281/#138a58/rgba() for success (lines 360-368, 392-400, 429-451). A success token already exists (SurfaceStatusSurfaceSuccess #26c281) and Box's real accent token is #5b4b8a (purple) — the periwinkle #5a7cf7 is off-brand and not cool-neutral aligned. Only brand/text/surface/stroke are tokenized.

No hover states anywhere — action buttons (line 298), bar/point buttons (line 343), and legend items are all interactive but have no :hover feedback despite SurfaceSurfaceHover/SurfaceSurfaceBrandHover tokens existing. Also no :active/pressed state (SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed unused) and no disabled state.

focus-visible is only defined for [part="point"] (line 370). The action buttons (line 298) have no focus-visible style, so keyboard focus on them falls back to the UA default with no brand focus ring — inconsistent keyboard affordance.

Points emit a point-selected event (line 142-150) but there is no persistent selected visual state or aria-pressed/aria-selected reflection — a selected bar looks identical to unselected ones, so selection is not communicated visually or to AT.

render() rewrites shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale on every attributeChangedCallback (line 228) and re-binds listeners. Any keyboard focus on a bar/action button is destroyed on re-render, and there are no live regions for summary/message updates, so dynamic data changes are silent to screen readers and disruptive to keyboard users.

Dispatched events use unprefixed names "action" and "point-selected" (lines 134, 144). Since they bubble and are composed, generic names risk collisions with host-app listeners; the design-system convention is a namespaced prefix (e.g. box-action / box-point-selected).

Spacing, padding and radii are arbitrary magic values (0.875rem, 0.95rem, 0.3rem, 0.42rem, 0.58rem gaps; 1rem/0.95rem/0.8rem/0.9rem radii) rather than a Blueprint spacing/radius scale, so rhythm is inconsistent with tokenized siblings.
No loading or error state is implemented; the only non-data path is a plain "No bar-chart data available." empty part (line 209). The `message` slot has no error styling variant.

summary: A functional, mostly-tokenized bar chart with clean XSS escaping and composed events, but it has real gaps: role="listitem" on <button> strips interactive semantics, it hijacks the native `title` attribute, hardcodes accent/success colors despite tokens existing, and lacks hover/active/disabled/selected states plus action-button focus rings.

A tidy, token-aware selection bar with clean event/reflection API, but it lacks a keyboard focus ring, disabled/active states, and live announcements, and several visual choices (off-grid spacing, hardcoded white color-mix, unstyled destructive tone) fall short of Box production polish.

issues.description: No :focus-visible styling anywhere. The action/clear buttons (part="action", part="clear") get only :hover rules (lines 300-304); keyboard users see no brand focus ring, so tabbing through the toolbar gives no visible focus indicator.

The selection count is never announced. The count/summary (part="count", line 235-239, text "N selected") has no aria-live region, so screen-reader users are not told how many items are selected when the bar renders or the count changes.

role="toolbar" (lines 161/183) is declared but the WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern is not implemented: buttons have no roving tabindex and no ArrowLeft/ArrowRight key handling. Every button is a separate tab stop, contradicting the toolbar role's expected single-tab-stop / arrow-key navigation.

No disabled state is supported. BulkAction has no disabled field and buttons are always enabled/clickable; a consumer cannot render an action as unavailable, and there is no :disabled or aria-disabled styling.

Dark-mode-breaking hardcoded color: --_obp-brand-soft is color-mix(in srgb, var(--_obp-brand) 12%, white 88%) (line 202). The literal 'white' does not adapt to a dark theme, so the primary action's soft background will be near-white on dark surfaces instead of tracking a token.

Destructive tone is unstyled. The action markup emits data-tone (line 169) and the type allows any tone, but only [data-tone="primary"] is styled (line 294). A 'delete'/'danger' action (like the delete action in the docs example) renders identical to neutral actions — no error/destructive color, unlike Box's Blueprint danger buttons.

Off-grid, hardcoded spacing and sizing rather than a Blueprint rhythm scale: values like padding 1rem 1.1rem, gap 0.85rem/0.65rem, font-size 0.8rem/1.4rem/0.92rem, and border-radius 1rem are not on a 4px grid and are not sourced from spacing/radius/type tokens.

No active/pressed feedback. Hover applies transform+border (lines 300-304) but there is no :active rule, even though the design system defines SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed (#004eaa) that a primary action press could use.

Full innerHTML re-render on every attributeChangedCallback (lines 91-93, 190) destroys and recreates all buttons. If a user has keyboard focus on an action and any attribute changes (e.g. count), focus is lost to the document body — a focus-management regression.

Redundant summary vs message. summaryText is hardcoded to `${count} selected` (line 141) and rendered alongside the free-form message. The docs example (count="3", message="3 items selected") therefore shows both "3 selected" and "3 items selected" stacked, which reads as duplicated copy.

Generic, un-namespaced event names "action" and "clear" (lines 110, 124) dispatched with bubbles+composed. These are prone to collision with other components/native-ish names on an ancestor listener; Box convention would namespace them (e.g. box-bulk-action).

Redundant aria-label on action buttons: aria-label duplicates the visible button text (line 170 vs line 172-173). It adds no information and can desync from the visible label if only one is translated.

render() rewrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML (including re-parsing the full <style> block) on every attributeChangedCallback, destroying and recreating the inner <button> on any change to disabled/label/size/tone. This re-parses CSS each time and, critically, loses DOM focus. State should be applied by mutating existing nodes, not clobbering innerHTML.

Focus is lost whenever an observed attribute changes because the inner button is recreated. Toggling disabled programmatically or updating label while the control has focus moves focus to body, breaking keyboard flow and screen-reader context.

No form participation and no type support. The inner button is hardcoded to type=button and the element is not form-associated (no ElementInternals / static formAssociated), so box-button can never submit or reset a form, a core design-system button expectation. No aria-pressed/toggle support either.
No loading/busy state despite it being a standard button state. There is no loading attribute, spinner, or aria-busy, so async actions cannot show progress or block re-clicks.

No custom event is dispatched; the component relies solely on the native click crossing the shadow boundary. There is no documented event contract and no way to emit/suppress activation when disabled or loading.

tone and size are typed as loose string with no validation. Unknown tones silently fall back to base primary styling (data-tone=primary matches no selector), and only size=small is styled; medium is implicit-default and there is no large.

focus-visible sets outline:none and substitutes a box-shadow ring, which is not rendered in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode, so the focus indicator disappears there. There is no @media (forced-colors) fallback, and the brand ring at 18% opacity is quite faint.

Elevation/shadows use hardcoded rgba(15,23,42,...) slate values and rgba(255,255,255,...) highlights rather than Box neutral/elevation tokens, bypassing the token system; the slate tint is not a Box cool-neutral.

Content is driven only by a label attribute (default Button); there is no slot for projected content or leading/trailing icons. The CSS declares gap:0.4em anticipating an icon, but no icon slot exists, so buttons are text-only.

summary: A visually polished, token-driven button with solid tone/size/hover/active/focus/disabled styling, but undermined by a full-innerHTML re-render on every attribute change that loses focus, plus no form participation, no loading state, and no custom event contract.

A well-structured radio-group-style toggle group with correct token usage, roving-tabindex keyboard nav, and a polished Blueprint look. It loses points for a dead disabled state, redundant/reentrant re-render logic on every value change, and a few spurious-event edge cases.

issues.description: The `[part="button"]:disabled` style block (lines 217-220) is dead code: the ButtonGroupOption type has only {label, value} with no `disabled` field, and no button is ever rendered with a `disabled` attribute. There is no way to disable an individual option, so the disabled state is unreachable.

No pressed/active (`:active`) state is implemented. Buttons transition on hover/focus/selected but give no immediate visual feedback on pointer-down, unlike the sibling button.ts which styles a brand-pressed state.

Setting the `value` property triggers a double render: the setter (lines 66-70) calls `this.setAttribute("value", ...)` — which fires attributeChangedCallback → render() — and then also calls `this.render()` directly. Every programmatic value change rebuilds the shadow DOM twice.

render() is reentrant: when the incoming value doesn't match an option it calls `setAttribute("value", selectedValue)` at line 92, which synchronously re-enters attributeChangedCallback → render() and sets innerHTML, before the outer render() then sets innerHTML again at line 124. The inner render's freshly attached listeners are discarded, wasting work on initial mount.

Keyboard navigation re-emits a `value-changed` event even when the value doesn't actually change. With a single option, ArrowRight/ArrowLeft compute nextIndex back to the same index (lines 250-253), then unconditionally set the value and dispatch value-changed (lines 268-276) with the identical value. The click handler guards against this (line 230) but the keydown handler does not.

Full `shadowRoot.innerHTML =` replacement plus re-attachment of all click/keydown listeners happens on every single arrow-key press during navigation (render() called via attributeChangedCallback). This is heavy for a keyboard interaction and relies on a queueMicrotask focus hack (lines 278-281) to restore focus to the newly-created button after the DOM is thrown away.

The component models a single-select radio group but offers no form participation: no ElementInternals/formAssociated, no `name` attribute, and the `value-changed` event name diverges from native form semantics. It cannot be submitted inside a form the way a real radio group can.

Elevation/highlight shadows are hardcoded (`rgba(15, 23, 42, ...)` and `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82)` at lines 159-160, 206-207) rather than driven by tokens, and the separated-layout `gap: 0.5rem` (line 136) plus radius `0.75rem` are magic numbers rather than spacing/radius tokens, so they won't track theme changes (e.g. box-dark).

summary: A genuinely polished, well-tokenized calendar with a full ARIA grid pattern and rich keyboard support, but it loses focus and fails to announce after month navigation, and re-rendering via innerHTML introduces double-render/double-dispatch bugs.

issues.description: Every render replaces shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale, so the aria-live="polite" title node (line 459) is destroyed and recreated on each month change. Screen readers only announce mutations to a *persisting* live region; a brand-new node with content present at insertion time is not announced, so month navigation is silent for AT users.

Clicking the prev/next nav buttons calls shiftMonth -> goToMonth -> setAttribute('month'), which re-renders and replaces the nav button DOM nodes. The focused nav button is destroyed, so keyboard focus falls back to <body>. A keyboard user paging through months loses their place after every activation (day-cell navigation restores focus via focusActive(), but nav buttons do not).

When the host has the disabled attribute, selectDate() and clampToEnabled() correctly no-op, but the prev/next nav <button>s (lines 456/460) are never given the disabled attribute or aria-disabled. A 'disabled' calendar still lets the user change months, which is inconsistent and misleading.

Enter/Space are handled in handleGridKeydown (lines 522-526) which calls selectDate, but the day cells are native <button>s that ALSO fire a native click -> selectDate on activation. preventDefault on keydown does not reliably suppress the native button activation for Enter, so selectDate can run twice and dispatch value-changed twice for a single keypress.

selectDate() unconditionally setAttribute('value') then setAttribute('month'); each triggers attributeChangedCallback -> render(), so a single selection performs two full innerHTML rebuilds. moveActive() has the same double-render when crossing a month boundary (setAttribute('month') renders, then this.render() renders again). Wasteful and re-attaches all listeners twice.

PageUp/PageDown shift by daysInMonth() of the currently displayed month (lines 501/507) rather than moving to the same day-of-month in the adjacent month. From a 31-day month this overshoots into the month after next (e.g. Jan 31 + 31 days = Mar 3), diverging from the APG date-picker convention of same-day month stepping.

No :active / pressed feedback anywhere. The design token SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed (#004eaa) exists specifically for this but is never referenced; nav buttons and day cells jump straight from hover to selected with no pressed state.

selectDate() dispatches value-changed even when the clicked/activated date already equals the current value (no equality guard against this.value), so re-selecting the selected day fires a spurious value-changed event.

Out-of-range and globally-disabled day cells use the native disabled attribute, which removes them from the accessibility tree and focus order. AT users cannot perceive that those dates exist-but-are-unavailable; aria-disabled="true" on still-focusable cells would communicate the min/max boundary better.

min/max attributes are consumed only via raw string comparison in isOutOfRange (lines 143-148) with no parseISO validation; a malformed min/max silently participates in lexicographic comparison instead of being ignored, and there is no re-validation of an existing value when min/max change.

summary: A purely presentational content card that wires up the main Blueprint color tokens correctly but ships light-mode-only hardcoded shadows, arbitrary (non-token) spacing/type, weak heading semantics, no interactive states, and a risky override of the native HTMLElement.title property.

issues.description: The component overrides the native HTMLElement.title property with custom get/set title() (lines 37-43) and uses 'title' as an observed attribute. Setting title="Quarterly Plan.pdf" (as the docs example does, examples.ts:110) makes the browser render a native OS tooltip on hover in addition to the visible heading, and shadowing the built-in title accessor is a footgun for consumers. A dedicated name like 'heading' should be the sole content attribute rather than colliding with the global title attribute.

No interactive states exist at all — no hover, focus-visible (brand focus ring), active, selected, disabled, or loading. The docs example renders a file card ("Quarterly Plan.pdf", updated by Morgan Lee) which in Box is a clickable/selectable surface, yet the element is a static <article> with no href/click affordance, no tabindex, and no keyboard interaction. There is no way to make the card actionable.

The box-shadow hardcodes light-mode colors: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.82) (a white top highlight) and 0 14px 28px rgba(15,23,42,0.04) (slate) at lines 82-84, and the background/border color-mix blends toward literal white (lines 74-81). None of these are token-driven, so the card will not adapt to the dark theme defined in src/foundations/tokens/box-dark.ts — the white inset highlight will look wrong on a dark surface.

Spacing, radius, and type are arbitrary hardcoded rem values not drawn from a Blueprint scale: gap 0.9rem / 0.35rem, padding 1rem, border-radius 1.1rem (lines 71-90), font-size 0.78rem and 1.04rem, letter-spacing -0.02em (lines 94-104). These off-scale values break the design system's spacing rhythm and type ramp; there are no --boe-token spacing/radius/font-size variables referenced.

The heading is rendered as <strong> (line 60) rather than a real heading element (h2/h3) or an element with role=heading + aria-level, so it carries no heading semantics for screen-reader navigation. The <article> landmark also has no accessible name (no aria-label/aria-labelledby tied to the title), and the eyebrow is an unlabeled decorative <span>.

render() rebuilds the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback (lines 49-62), re-parsing the full <style> block on each attribute change instead of updating only the affected text nodes — wasteful and it re-creates the <slot>, which can disrupt slotted-content state.

No custom events are dispatched and there is no interaction to emit them for. Combined with the redundant, mutually-referential heading/title getters (heading falls back to title at line 30, title falls back to heading at line 38), the public API is confusing: two attributes ('heading' and 'title') control the same rendered string with no clear precedence documented.

summary: A visually decorated carousel that uses design tokens and reflects attributes correctly, but its accessibility is substantially broken: a misused tab pattern, no keyboard arrow navigation, and focus loss on every slide change. Solid API surface undermined by an over-decorated, non-Blueprint-flat visual treatment and a full-innerHTML re-render pattern.

issues.description: The pagination dots use role="tab" inside role="tablist" but there is no role="tabpanel", no aria-controls linking tabs to content, and selection is expressed with aria-current="true/false" instead of aria-selected. Screen readers announce a tab widget with no panels and no valid selected state (carousel.ts lines 335-348). Either drop the tab roles for plain buttons or implement the full tabs pattern.

No keyboard arrow-key navigation. The ARIA tablist/tab roles imply Left/Right (or Up/Down) arrow handling with roving tabindex, but every dot is in the natural tab order and arrow keys do nothing; the carousel itself also has no keyboard affordance beyond tabbing to each button. No keydown listeners exist in render() (lines 361-368).

Focus is destroyed on every navigation. setIndex()/changeBy() call render(), which overwrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML (line 125), so the Next/Previous button or dot the user just activated is removed from the DOM and focus falls back to document body — keyboard users lose their place after each slide change.

Slide changes are not announced. The [part="content"]/[part="viewport"] region has no aria-live (e.g. aria-live="polite") and no role, so assistive-tech users get no feedback that the active slide's title/description changed when they press Next or a dot.

Line 267 references --boe-token-surface-item-surface-hover, which is not a registered token (the registry only defines SurfaceItemSurfaceSelected and SurfaceSurfaceHover). The hover background therefore always resolves to the hardcoded fallback #eef4fb instead of a themed value, so hover state won't track dark theme.

Heavily decorated rather than Blueprint-flat: numerous hardcoded rgba() shadows (e.g. lines 146-147, 180-182, 199-200) and multi-stop decorative gradients, plus spacing/radius expressed as magic rem values (1.2rem, 1.05rem, 2.45rem) instead of a token spacing/radius scale. Box's modernized system is comparatively flat and rhythm-driven.

No :active/pressed state on the previous/next buttons or dots, even though pressed brand tokens exist (SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed). Dots also have no :hover state — only the selected dot changes appearance — so unselected dots give no interactive feedback before click.

Double render per interaction: setIndex() (line 100) sets the "value" attribute, which fires attributeChangedCallback → render() (lines 70-76), and then setIndex() also calls render() directly (line 102). Every navigation rebuilds the entire shadow DOM twice.

The value property setter reflects and renders but does not dispatch value-changed (only setIndex does), an undocumented asymmetry. There are also no imperative next()/previous()/goTo() methods, so programmatic control requires manipulating value directly. The docs example (examples.ts lines 114-118) passes items with an id field that CarouselItem does not declare and the renderer ignores.

summary: A thoughtful, mostly solid radiogroup pill filter with correct token usage, roving tabindex and keyboard support, but it has a real keyboard-unreachable edge case when the selected value is disabled, lacks a pressed/active state and arrow wrap/Home-End, and can steal focus on unrelated re-renders.

issues.description: If `value` is set (via attribute/property) to a disabled option, `tabbableValue` becomes that disabled value, but the tabindex expression requires `!option.disabled`, so the matching pill gets tabindex=-1 and no other pill matches. Result: every pill is tabindex=-1 and the entire radiogroup becomes unreachable by keyboard. The tabbable-fallback should skip disabled options (e.g. fall back to first selectable when the selected value is disabled).

No active/pressed state is implemented. The design system exposes --boe-token-surface-surface-brand-pressed (SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed #004eaa) and a hover variant, but there is no `:active` rule on `[part~="pill"]`, so pressing a pill gives no tactile feedback distinct from hover/checked.

Arrow navigation clamps at the ends via Math.max/Math.min (no wraparound) and there is no Home/End support. The APG radiogroup/toolbar pattern expects arrow keys to wrap and typically Home/End to jump to first/last enabled option; both are missing.

render() refocuses the checked pill whenever `hadFocus && valueInternal` is true. Because attributeChangedCallback re-renders on any observed attribute change (e.g. an external `options` or `label` update), a re-render while focus merely happens to be inside the shadow root will yank focus back onto the checked pill, potentially stealing/moving focus during interactions the user did not initiate.

The `value` setter both calls render() and calls setAttribute('value', nextValue), and setAttribute synchronously triggers attributeChangedCallback which sets valueInternal again and calls render() a second time — every programmatic value assignment double-renders (rebuilding innerHTML and re-attaching all listeners twice).

Sizing uses arbitrary hardcoded values off any spacing rhythm: gap 0.4rem, padding 0.4rem 0.85rem, font-size 0.84rem. These are not aligned to a consistent spacing/type scale (0.84rem/0.85rem are oddly specific), reducing Blueprint rhythm consistency versus sibling components.

The focus-visible indicator is a box-shadow ring at only 26% brand opacity (color-mix ... 26%). On light surfaces this soft, low-contrast ring may not meet the 3:1 non-text contrast expectation for a clearly visible focus indicator (WCAG 2.4.11/1.4.11); a more opaque or layered ring would be safer.

The full shadow innerHTML is regenerated and all per-button click/keydown listeners are re-attached on every render (including on each arrow keypress and every attribute change), which is wasteful and relies on the manual refocus hack to preserve focus; a persistent DOM with delegated listeners would be more robust.

Each data point is a native <button> but is given role="listitem" (chart-panel.ts:185), which overrides the implicit button role. Screen readers announce these as list items, not activatable buttons, so the click-to-select interaction (point-selected event) is not discoverable to AT users. Wrap bars in a listitem container or drop the role and use aria-label alone; do not put role=listitem on an interactive button.

Interactivity states are largely missing. Action buttons ([part="action"], lines 288-303) have NO :focus-visible, :hover, or :active styles at all, so keyboard focus on them is invisible (fails focus-ring requirement). Points have :focus-visible but no :hover or :active. There is no disabled, loading, or persistent selected state anywhere, even though the component emits point-selected — the clicked point never visually reflects selection (no aria-pressed / selected styling).

Accent and success tones are hardcoded rather than tokenized. #5a7cf7/#3f62ea (accent) is not even a Box token — the Blueprint accent token is SurfaceStatusSurfaceAccent (#5b4b8a light / #a892f0 dark). Success is hardcoded #26c281 / rgba(38,194,129) instead of --boe-token-surface-status-surface-success. These bypass the token layer entirely and cannot adapt to theme overrides.

No dark-theme support. Backgrounds are built with color-mix against literal white (e.g. white 92%, white 4% at lines 232, 328, 337-342, 390-401) and light-blue tints (#eef4fb), and the primary action uses color: white (line 302). In a dark theme these stay light while token-driven text (#1f1e1b fallback) stays dark, producing unreadable contrast. Dark tokens exist (box-dark.ts) but are ignored.

The component overrides the `title` property/attribute (lines 103-109, observedAttributes). `title` is a global HTML attribute that renders a native browser tooltip, so setting title="Usage" on the host produces an unintended hover tooltip and collides with the inherited HTMLElement.title semantics. Use a non-reserved name like `heading` / `panel-title`.

Text color fallbacks are stale: --boe-token-text-text falls back to #1f1e1b (lines 257, 265, 370) but the actual Box default token value is #222222 (box-defaults.ts:37). When the token variable is unset the component renders a slightly different neutral than the design system specifies.

render() replaces the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback (line 218) and re-attaches all listeners. Any attribute change (e.g. updating summary) destroys and rebuilds the DOM, dropping keyboard focus on a point/action mid-interaction. Fine for static panels but brittle for live-updating charts.

Emitted events use bare, unnamespaced names "action" and "point-selected" (lines 134, 145) and are non-cancelable with no default prevented behavior. "action" in particular is generic and collision-prone on a bubbling/composed event; a box- prefix would match design-system conventions.

Negative point values render inconsistently: the bar height is clamped to 0 via Math.max(point.value,0) (line 190) but [part="point-value"] displays the raw negative number (line 192) and maxValue via reduce ignores negatives, so a dataset with negative values shows a flat bar next to a negative label with no visual encoding of sign.

summary: A visually styled but systemically shallow insights pattern: it wires up token variables with fallbacks and escapes all input, but hardcodes off-brand accent/success colors, has no dark-theme handling, omits most interactive states (no focus ring on action buttons, no hover/active/selected/disabled), and breaks button semantics by forcing role=listitem onto its clickable points.

A thin wrapper around a native checkbox that leans on accent-color rather than a Box-drawn control, missing indeterminate/error/hover states and form participation. A focus-loss-on-toggle re-render bug and double rendering undercut the otherwise-decent native-input a11y baseline.

issues.description: Toggling the checkbox loses keyboard focus. The change handler (lines 134-138) calls setAttribute/removeAttribute('checked'), which triggers attributeChangedCallback -> render() (lines 62-68, 70-125). render() rebuilds shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale, destroying and replacing the <input> the user just activated, so focus is dropped after every keyboard/click toggle.

Redundant double render. The `checked` property setter (lines 27-36) both mutates the 'checked' attribute (which fires attributeChangedCallback -> render) AND calls this.render() directly, causing two full innerHTML rebuilds per property set. render() also re-parses the entire template and re-attaches a change listener on every state change instead of building the DOM once and updating properties.

Not a form-associated custom element. It lacks static formAssociated, ElementInternals, and any `value`/`name` attributes, so the checkbox never participates in form submission or FormData — a fundamental gap for a form control. observedAttributes (line 13) omits value, name, required, and indeterminate.

No indeterminate state. A production checkbox must support the tri-state indeterminate visual/ARIA (input.indeterminate). There is no attribute, property, or rendering path for it, which also blocks correct parent/child group semantics used by the sibling box-checkbox-group.

Missing hover, active/pressed, and error/invalid states. Only focus-visible (lines 101-104) and disabled (lines 110-113) are styled; there is no distinct hover treatment on the box, no pressed feedback, and no error styling despite the design system exposing --boe-token-surface-status-surface-error for invalid form fields.

Relies on the native control via `accent-color` (line 97) rather than a Box-drawn checkbox, so there is no custom checkmark, no stroke token for the box outline (--boe-token-stroke-stroke), and no controlled border-radius. Spacing is off-scale (gap: 0.6rem, line 86) and the label uses font-weight: 500 (line 107) where Box body labels are typically 400, so it does not match Blueprint fidelity.

Redundant/duplicate labeling. The visible <span part='label'> is already associated via the wrapping <label> (lines 115-124), yet an aria-label with the same text is also set on the input (line 119). This double-labels the control and can desync from the visible text; the aria-label should be dropped in favor of the visible label association.

Non-standard event contract. It dispatches only a custom 'checked-changed' event (lines 139-145) and no native-style 'change'/'input', and the detail payload carries `checked` but no `value`, making it awkward to consume in standard form tooling.

Silent default label. label getter returns 'Checkbox' when the attribute is absent (line 51), so an unlabeled instance renders a meaningless visible+aria label 'Checkbox' instead of surfacing a developer error or rendering label-less.

Toggling any checkbox re-renders the entire shadow DOM and loses focus. The change handler calls this.setAttribute("value", ...) (line 234); "value" is in observedAttributes (line 19), so attributeChangedCallback fires and calls render() (line 96), which wipes shadowRoot.innerHTML (line 121) and rebuilds all inputs. The just-clicked input is destroyed, so keyboard users toggling with Space lose focus after every selection — a serious usability/a11y regression and wasteful full re-render on each interaction.

The option gradients and hover/selected backgrounds mix surface tokens with literal `white` (e.g. lines 162-163, 176-177, 186-187: `color-mix(... surface-secondary 88%, white 12%)`). In the box-dark theme the surface tokens become near-black but the hardcoded `white` stays white, so every option tile washes out to a pale gradient in dark mode. Backgrounds should mix against a surface token, not a hardcoded color, to re-theme correctly.

Focus is styled with :focus-within (line 194), not :focus-visible, so the brand outline appears on plain mouse clicks, not just keyboard focus. Unlike the sibling checkbox.ts (which correctly uses [part="input"]:focus-visible, line 101), the group shows a persistent ring on pointer interaction. No :active/pressed state is provided either.
No error/invalid state is implemented despite this being a form control. There is no error attribute, no aria-invalid, and no error styling/token usage, so validation feedback cannot be surfaced.

Elevation/shadows are hardcoded non-token colors: the selected tile uses `rgba(255,255,255,0.82)` and `rgba(15,23,42,0.04)` box-shadows (lines 190-191). These bypass the design-token system entirely and won't adapt across themes. Spacing values (0.82rem, 0.65rem, 0.95rem radius) are also off the Blueprint 4px rhythm rather than tokenized spacing/radius.

The fieldset/legend grouping is good, but the group exposes no aria-describedby / help-text association and no way to mark required. Options rely solely on native checkbox semantics; there is no indeterminate or select-all affordance for a composite group.

Options with duplicate `value` strings would all be checked/unchecked together because selection is tracked by value membership (this.valueInternal.includes(option.value), line 112) and re-derived from :checked inputs; there is no uniqueness guard. Also the value setter (lines 71-75) calls render() and then setAttribute("value") which triggers attributeChangedCallback→render() again, double-rendering.

The dispatched event is named "value-changed" with detail.value, which is reasonable, but there is no cancelable/change semantics and the value attribute is always reflected as a JSON string; consumers must JSON.parse the attribute. No form-association (ElementInternals) so the group does not participate in native form submission.

summary: A visually polished token-aware group with correct fieldset/legend semantics, but it re-renders the whole shadow DOM on every toggle (losing focus), washes out in dark mode due to gradients mixed against hardcoded white, and uses :focus-within instead of :focus-visible with no error/active states.

A well-built, token-driven chip with proper attribute reflection, escaping, and a clever focus-restoration mechanism, but it has genuine ARIA structural problems (nested interactive elements, listitem without a list) and a few polish gaps. Solidly above average, not yet flawless production quality.

issues.description: When a chip is both selectable and removable, the remove <button> is rendered inside the container span that carries role="button" (nested interactive content). A button inside a button is invalid ARIA and breaks focus/AT semantics — the inner control should not live inside a role="button" ancestor.

Non-selectable chips are given role="listitem" (line 234), but nothing guarantees a role="list" ancestor. The docs example (examples.ts line 180) renders two chips with no list wrapper, so these listitems are orphaned — invalid per ARIA and announced inconsistently. A neutral role (or none) would be safer, with the list responsibility documented for the host.

Focus indicators use box-shadow with outline:none (lines 187-190, 218-221). box-shadow is not rendered in Windows forced-colors/high-contrast mode, leaving keyboard focus completely invisible there. Pair the ring with a transparent outline (e.g. outline: 2px solid transparent) so a visible focus indicator survives forced-colors.

No active/pressed state is implemented for the interactive (selectable) chip or the remove button. Hover, focus-visible, selected, and disabled are covered, but there is no :active affordance so clicks feel unresponsive relative to Box's pressed tokens (SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed exists and is unused).

A dedicated selected-surface token exists (SurfaceItemSurfaceSelected = #f2f7fd) but the selected state instead re-derives its background via color-mix on the brand token (lines 176-180). Using the intended token would keep selected chips consistent with other selected surfaces in the system.

Spacing, radius stops, and typography are hardcoded arbitrary rems (padding 0.28rem/0.35rem/0.7rem, gap 0.35rem, font-size 0.82rem, icon 0.7rem). The token set has no spacing scale, so this is partly unavoidable, but the off-grid 0.82rem font-size and mixed rhythm values read as un-systematized rather than snapped to a consistent scale.

render() rebuilds the entire shadow DOM via innerHTML and re-attaches all event listeners on every attributeChangedCallback (lines 117, 133, 247-260). During upgrade this fires once per present observed attribute, causing several redundant full re-renders; a class/attribute-diff update path would avoid the churn and the need for the focus-restoration workaround.

The dispatched events are the generic, un-namespaced names "remove" and "select" (lines 95, 109). "remove" in particular collides conceptually with HTMLElement.prototype.remove() and risks ambiguity in host listeners; a namespaced convention (e.g. box-chip-remove / box-chip-select) would be more robust and consistent with a design-system component.

Avatar and overflow backgrounds hardcode the literal color `white` inside color-mix (`color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-brand) 12%, white 88%)` and `... surface-secondary 82%, white 18%`), while the surrounding border uses the theme-aware `--boe-token-surface-surface` token. In dark mode `SurfaceSurface` is #1c1c1c (verified in box-dark.ts), so avatars render near-white with a dark border on a dark page — the fill never adapts to the theme. Backgrounds should mix against `--boe-token-surface-surface`, not `white`.

No fallback when an avatar image fails to load. `render()` emits `` when `collaborator.src` is present and never renders the initials alongside; a broken/404 src leaves an empty circle instead of falling back to initials. There is no `onerror` handling or CSS background-initial behind the image.

Hover and focus-visible are collapsed into a single rule (lines 174-181) with identical treatment (translateY(-2px) + brand box-shadow ring). This means mere hover paints the brand 'focus ring', which is visually indistinguishable from keyboard focus, and there is no separate `:active`/pressed state. Box buttons keep hover and focus-visible distinct.

The focus ring can be visually occluded by neighboring avatars. Avatars overlap via `margin-inline-start:-0.55rem` with a static inline `z-index:${visible.length - index}`; on hover/focus only `transform: translateY(-2px)` is applied and z-index is not raised, so a focused non-last avatar keeps its lower stacking order and the right side of its box-shadow ring is painted over by the next avatar. Raise z-index (or set position/isolation) on :hover/:focus-visible.

Every attributeChangedCallback triggers a full `shadowRoot.innerHTML = ...` rebuild (including re-parsing the entire <style> block) and re-attaches all listeners. Beyond being inefficient, replacing the DOM destroys focus: if a collaborators/max/label update fires while an avatar button is focused, focus is lost to the body with no restoration.

Sizing and typography use arbitrary magic values rather than Blueprint spacing/type tokens: `inline-size/block-size: 2.1rem`, `margin-inline-start: -0.55rem`, `font-size: 0.78rem` (avatars) and `0.86rem` (empty state), `box-shadow ...3px`. None reference a spacing or type-scale token, so rhythm drifts from the rest of the system.

`initialsFromName` derives initials with `segment[0]`, which indexes a single UTF-16 code unit. Names beginning with an astral/emoji or combined grapheme (surrogate pair) yield a broken half-character rather than a clean initial; use `Array.from(segment)[0]` or Intl.Segmenter.

Each avatar button sets both `aria-label` and `title` to the collaborator name (and the overflow chip both aria-label and title to 'N more'). The redundant title can cause some screen readers to double-announce, and the native tooltip duplicates the accessible name; the title adds no information beyond the aria-label.

summary: A clean, accessible avatar-pile pattern with solid semantics (role=group, native buttons, keyboard-ready) and a sensible reflected API with select/overflow events, but visual fidelity is undermined by hardcoding `white` in color-mix backgrounds that break dark mode, plus gaps around image-load fallback, occluded focus rings, and merged hover/focus states.

A functional, token-aware color picker with solid attribute reflection and a Box-blue focus ring, but it is undermined by an accessibility bug (buttons re-roled as listitems), a re-render-during-drag interaction bug, and missing active/error states plus non-token spacing and default color.

issues.description: Each swatch is a native <button> but is given role="listitem" (lines 125-131), which overrides the implicit button role in the accessibility tree. Screen readers announce these as plain list items with no interactive affordance, and aria-pressed is invalid/ignored on a listitem role, so the selected-color state is not conveyed. Use a radiogroup/radio or button toggle-group pattern instead of list/listitem.

The native <input type="color"> 'input' handler (line 262) calls emitValueChanged -> setAttribute("value") -> attributeChangedCallback -> render(), which rebuilds the entire shadow DOM via innerHTML on every input event. Because the OS color picker fires continuous 'input' events while the user drags, the <input> element is destroyed and recreated mid-interaction, breaking dragging/focus. render() should not run a full innerHTML rebuild on live value changes.

No error/invalid state is implemented: there is no aria-invalid, no error styling, and the SurfaceStatusSurfaceError token is unused, despite this being a form field that commonly needs validation feedback. No :active/pressed state is provided either (SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed token is unused), so there is no press feedback on swatches or the control.

The default/fallback color in normalizeHex and valueInternal is hardcoded to #3b82f6 (lines 18, 22, 30), a generic Tailwind blue, not the Box brand blue #0061d5 (SurfaceSurfaceBrand). Any invalid input or unset value silently snaps to an off-brand color, and it also swallows invalid swatch values into that same off-brand default rather than skipping them.

Spacing and radius use arbitrary rem/px literals not tied to any spacing or radius scale (e.g. gap 0.7rem/0.45rem, padding 0.45rem 0.7rem, border-radius 0.7rem/0.55rem/999px, sizes 2.1rem/1.7rem). Blueprint rhythm should come from a shared scale; these ad-hoc values reduce consistency with the rest of the system.

The field label is rendered as uppercase, font-weight 700, letter-spacing 0.08em (lines 155-161). Box Blueprint form-field labels are regular-weight sentence-case, so this stylized treatment deviates from the design system's actual form label styling.

The swatch group has no roving tabindex or arrow-key navigation; every swatch is a separate tab stop, and there is no keyboard model (arrow keys) expected of a color-selection group. There is also no live region announcing the newly selected value to assistive tech.

Programmatically setting .value renders twice: the setter calls this.render() (line 83) and also setAttribute("value") which triggers attributeChangedCallback -> render() again (lines 90-96). Redundant full-DOM rebuilds on a single assignment.

The native input type=color only accepts 6-digit hex, and normalizeHex rejects shorthand (#fff), 8-digit/alpha, and named colors, coercing them to the default. The public value API silently discards otherwise-reasonable color strings without any validation feedback to the caller.

summary: A thin wrapper over a native input+datalist that styles cleanly with tokens but has serious correctness bugs: it destroys the input (losing focus/caret) on every keystroke, never maps the selected label back to the option value, and lacks real combobox ARIA. Not production quality.

issues.description: The input's 'input' handler calls this.setAttribute('value', nextValue), which triggers attributeChangedCallback -> render() -> shadowRoot.innerHTML = ... This tears down and recreates the <input> element on every keystroke, so the field loses focus and caret position while typing, making the component effectively unusable for text entry (lines 76-82, 96-166, 169-180).

value/label mapping is broken: each datalist <option> is rendered with value=option.label and the real key stored in data-option-value (line 92), but data-option-value is never read anywhere. Selecting 'PDF' sets the component value to the label 'PDF', not the intended 'pdf'. The value getter therefore returns the display label, never the option's value.

No true combobox semantics. Relies solely on native <input list> + <datalist>, which has inconsistent/limited screen-reader support across browsers and exposes no role=combobox, aria-expanded, aria-controls, or aria-activedescendant. There is no managed listbox, no keyboard arrow navigation over options beyond the browser's native datalist behavior, and no announcement of the number of matches.

Disabled is styled ([part=input]:disabled at lines 150-153) but unreachable: 'disabled' is not in observedAttributes (line 18) and the input is never given a disabled attribute. There is also no error state, no loading state, and no visible selected state, so several expected form-field states are absent.

The visible label is force-uppercased with letter-spacing (lines 111-114) and rendered as decorative micro-caps. While it is associated via the wrapping <label>, the treatment departs from Box Blueprint field-label conventions and reduces legibility; there is no aria-required/aria-invalid support for validation states.

render() rebuilds the entire shadow DOM (innerHTML assignment) on every attribute change and re-attaches an event listener each time, which is wasteful and fragile. A hardcoded datalist id ('combobox-options', lines 160/164) is safe only because each instance has its own shadow root, but the full re-render pattern is the root cause of the keystroke bug above.

Mostly token-driven with sensible fallbacks and a brand focus ring, but uses a decorative vertical gradient background (lines 124-129) and an oversized 0.7rem radius that read as bespoke rather than Blueprint; spacing/rhythm is ad hoc (0.45rem gap, 0.6rem/0.85rem padding) rather than tied to a spacing scale token.

render() rewrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on EVERY attributeChangedCallback. In a people-picker, toggling `selected` is the most common mutation, and it tears down and rebuilds the whole subtree (including the inner role=option div). If that option currently has DOM focus, replacing it drops focus to — a real focus-management/keyboard-nav bug in a listbox. It also destroys and re-creates the , forcing the avatar to re-download and flash on every selection change. A picker should mutate attributes/classes in place, not re-parse innerHTML.

Selected and avatar backgrounds are built with `color-mix(... var(--boe-token-surface-surface-brand) X%, white Y%)`, hardcoding `white` as the mix partner. The design system ships a dedicated selected token `--boe-token-surface-item-surface-selected` with a proper dark value (#14335a in box-dark.ts), used by persona/menu-item/dropdown/etc. By mixing against literal `white` this component both ignores the convention token AND renders a light blue tint in dark theme, defeating dark-mode theming.

The `[part=item]:hover` background rule fires even when the item is disabled — hovering a disabled row still lifts the background to surface-hover while the cursor shows not-allowed, an inconsistent state. Hover should be suppressed via a `:not([data-disabled="true"])` guard. There is also no active/pressed state (e.g. surface-surface-brand-pressed) for click feedback.

Spacing/radii use arbitrary off-grid rem values rather than the Blueprint 4px grid: gap 0.7rem (11.2px), padding 0.5rem/0.65rem (10.4px), radius 0.6rem (9.6px), avatar 2.1rem (33.6px), gap 0.1rem (1.6px). None land on the 4px rhythm and none are drawn from spacing tokens, giving subtly inconsistent rhythm versus other components.

The `value` getter `this.getAttribute("value") ?? this.email ?? this.name` has a dead fallback: the `email` getter always returns a string (`getAttribute(...) ?? ""`), never null/undefined, so `?? this.name` can never execute. When the value attribute is absent and email is empty, `value` resolves to "" and choose() dispatches `detail.value: ""` instead of falling back to the name as apparently intended.

When `name` is empty the name span renders empty and only the email (if any) contributes to the option's accessible name; with neither set the option has an empty accessible name while still showing a "?" avatar. There is no aria-label fallback, so a nameless contact row is announced as a blank option.

summary: A clean, well-structured option component with a solid attribute/reflection/event API and good baseline a11y (role=option, aria-selected/aria-disabled, keyboard, tabindex), but it re-renders its entire innerHTML on every attribute change — dropping focus and reflashing the avatar when `selected` toggles — and its white-based color-mix backgrounds ignore the dedicated selected token and break in dark mode.

A capable pattern with disciplined token usage, a rich event/method API, and roving-tabindex keyboard support, but undermined by a real XSS hole in error rendering, focus-stealing on every render, and several ARIA gaps that keep it below production quality.

issues.description:
The error message is injected unescaped: `errorMarkup = state?.error ? '

' + state.error.message + '

' : ''` (line 419). Every other piece of dynamic content (folder name, item name, crumb name) goes through escapeHtml, but error.message does not. A transport/backend error string containing markup executes as HTML in the shadow root — an XSS/injection vector. Wrap it in escapeHtml().

render() unconditionally re-focuses whenever this.focusItemId is set: the queueMicrotask block at lines 712-719 calls target.focus() on every render.
