Line 165 sets aria-label to `this.label || "Help text"` on the role="note" container while the same label is ALSO rendered as visible <strong part="label"> content (line 80/168). When a label is present a screen reader reads it twice (region name + content); when absent, the note is named the generic "Help text" which conveys nothing. The container should not carry a duplicate aria-label.

role="note" is a questionable semantic for inline field help. role="note" implies a standalone parenthetical/aside region; inline help text describing an input is better exposed as plain described-by text (or role="alert"/status for error tones), not a note region. As written it adds an unnecessary region semantic without the association that would make it useful.

Confusing, redundant dual API for the same text. `message` and `description` are two attributes/props that fall back to each other (message getter -> message attr ?? this.description; description getter -> description attr ?? message attr), and both are rendered into a single span given two part names `part="description message"` (line 169). Two names for one concept invites ambiguity about precedence and adds no capability; a single `message` (or `description`) attribute would be cleaner.

Misleading icon fallback. Line 87 `resolveDesignIcon(iconName) ?? "i"` falls back to the literal text "i" for ALL tones, including error/warning (which requested the "alert" icon) and success ("checkmark"). If the design system lacks the icon, an error message renders a benign "i" glyph, misrepresenting severity. Fallback glyph should be tone-appropriate (e.g. "!" for error/warning).

Spacing/typography uses arbitrary magic numbers rather than a Blueprint rhythm scale: gap 0.45rem, font-size 0.86rem, margin-top 0.08rem, width/height 1.15rem, content gap 0.15rem. These are hand-tuned values not drawn from any spacing/type token scale, so they won't stay consistent with the rest of the system if the scale changes.

No events dispatched. Acceptable for a static text component, but note the component is purely presentational with no programmatic signal on tone/message change, unlike siblings (alert.ts dispatches open-changed/dismiss). Fine as-is, flagged only for completeness.

summary: A clean, presentational help-text element with correct --boe-token-* usage and tone variants, but it is functionally weak as an accessibility helper: no live region for error/warning announcements, no aria-describedby association with any field, and a redundant/generic aria-label. API carries a confusing duplicate message/description surface.

issues.description: render() rewrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback and on design-system-change. Because the <button> element is destroyed and recreated, any keyboard/mouse focus on it is lost whenever an attribute (e.g. tone) changes or the design system re-renders. A focused icon button that toggles state would silently drop focus, breaking keyboard flow. Should update in place or preserve/restore focus.

The component dispatches no custom events. It relies solely on the native shadow <button> click bubbling out (composed). There is no semantic press/toggle event, so consumers cannot distinguish an icon-button action without attaching to raw clicks, and there is no event surface for a toggled/selected state.

No loading state and no selected/toggle (aria-pressed) state are implemented, though icon buttons in a design system are commonly toggles (e.g. favorite, pin) and async triggers. observedAttributes is only [disabled, icon, label, tone] — there is no 'selected'/'pressed'/'loading' attribute or corresponding styling/ARIA.

The danger tone (data-tone="danger") has base and :hover styles but no :active pressed state, unlike the sibling button.ts which defines a danger :active. Pressing a danger icon button gives no pressed feedback beyond the hover color.

Default tone is 'secondary' with only 'primary' and 'danger' styled variants, but the sibling box-button uses 'neutral'/'primary'/'danger'. The inconsistent default tone name ('secondary' vs 'neutral') across two actions components is a system-coherence gap. Also there is no size attribute/variant, whereas box-button exposes size=small.

The danger tone's :focus-visible ring uses the brand blue color (surface-surface-brand at 18%) rather than a danger-tinted ring; button.ts tints the danger focus ring with the error color. The focus indicator therefore does not reflect the destructive tone.

When no label is set, aria-label falls back to the generic 'Icon button', which is not a meaningful accessible name. An icon-only control with a placeholder label provides poor screen-reader semantics; label should arguably be required or warn.

The default icon fallback is the text bullet '•' (icon getter returns '•'). When no icon resolves and none is supplied, the button renders a bullet glyph rather than a neutral placeholder, which looks unintentional.

summary: A visually polished icon button with strong --boe-token usage, Blueprint radius/gradients, and design-system re-render support, but held back by a focus-losing full innerHTML re-render, no custom events, and missing toggle/loading states.

issues.description: The docs example (docs-site/examples.ts:409) sets `label="Empty folder"`, but the component has no `label` attribute or property and does not list it in observedAttributes (['asset','caption','message','shape','title']). The label is silently ignored: figcaption renders empty and aria-label falls back to the generic 'Illustration'. The documented API and the implemented API diverge.

The <figure> carries role="img" (line 294), which makes all descendants presentational. The visible <figcaption> title/caption text is therefore not exposed to assistive tech; only aria-label is, and aria-label is `title || message || 'Illustration'` — it can never convey both title AND caption together. A user with both set loses the caption for screen readers.

aria-label falls back to the non-descriptive literal 'Illustration' when neither title nor message is provided (line 294) — exactly the state produced by the docs example — giving screen-reader users no meaningful description.

The `caption` and `message` attributes are redundant and circular: caption getter returns getAttribute('caption') ?? this.message, message getter returns getAttribute('message') ?? getAttribute('caption'), and render() only ever reads this.message (line 100). The `caption` property/observed attribute is effectively dead-weight indirection with confusing dual naming.

Shadows and highlights are hardcoded cool-slate values (rgba(15,23,42,0.08/0.1/0.12) and rgba(255,255,255,...) at lines 161,169,183-185,211,229-230,259) rather than token-derived, and spacing/radius use arbitrary one-off rems (1.9rem, 2.55rem, 5.95rem, 3.2rem, 6.9rem, radius 2rem/1rem) with no Blueprint spacing/radius scale.

The generative cloud/stack/spark shapes are tinted with --boe-token-surface-surface-brand, but the design system defines a dedicated illustration token (SurfaceIllustrationSurfaceBoxNeutral / --boe-token-surface-illustration-surface-box-neutral) which the registered SVG assets use. The fallback shapes should use that same illustration token for consistency.

data-shape on the figure (line 294) reflects the raw unvalidated shape attribute, while renderShape() defaults any unknown shape to 'cloud'. An unknown value like shape='banana' renders a cloud but reports data-shape="banana", so the attribute lies about what is drawn.

The component overrides the native HTMLElement `title` property/attribute (lines 62-68, observed at line 22). Setting `title` still triggers the browser's native tooltip in addition to the component's use, and shadowing a global attribute is surprising; a namespaced attribute (e.g. heading/headline) would avoid the collision.

render() rewrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML — including the full <style> block — on every attributeChangedCallback and design-system-change event (lines 105,79-81). The stylesheet is re-parsed on each mutation; a cached constructable stylesheet plus targeted DOM updates would be more robust and efficient.

No custom events are dispatched (e.g. on asset load/resolve failure). When resolveDesignIllustration returns null for an unregistered asset it silently falls back to a shape with no signal to consumers, making misconfigured asset names hard to detect.

summary: A presentational illustration element with clean HTML-escaping and reasonable color-token usage, but its documented `label` attribute is unimplemented, its role=\"img\" markup hides the visible caption from assistive tech, and it leans on hardcoded shadows/arbitrary spacing rather than a Blueprint scale. Non-interactive, so state coverage is largely N/A.

A competently structured modal with a clean headless-controller split and a solid attribute/property/event API, but it falls short of Box production quality on modal accessibility (no focus trap, no Escape, no focus restoration), interaction states (no hover/active), and one invented token that resolves to a non-Box fallback color.

issues.description: The dialog sets role=dialog + aria-modal=true but implements no focus trap. Tab/Shift+Tab escape into the background page, and there is no `inert`/aria-hidden applied to the rest of the document. A modal pattern must trap focus within the dialog.

No Escape-key handler. There is a keydown listener only on the recipient input (for Enter/comma); pressing Escape anywhere in the dialog does nothing. Modals are expected to close on Escape and dispatch the cancel event.
Focus is moved into the recipient input on open (line 147) but is never restored to the triggering element on close/submit. close() and submit() just set this.open=false, dropping focus to <body>.

Line 263 uses `var(--boe-token-text-text-danger, #b3261e)`, but no `TextTextDanger` token exists in box-defaults.ts. The actual Box error color is `SurfaceStatusSurfaceError` (#ed3757); the hardcoded fallback #b3261e is a Material-Design red, so error text renders off-brand in every theme.

Pill background (line 215) is `color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-secondary,...) 70%, white 30%)`. Hardcoding `white` in the mix (rather than a surface token) produces a washed-out light pill that breaks against a dark surface in box-dark; the backdrop rgba(15,23,42,.45) and box-shadow slate values (lines 171, 185) are likewise hardcoded rather than token-derived.

No hover or active/pressed states on any interactive element. The submit button never uses `--boe-token-surface-surface-brand-hover` (#0057c0) / `-pressed` (#004eaa) which exist in the token set; cancel button, role select, and pill-remove buttons have no hover feedback either.

The controller is never reset() on cancel or success — close() and submit() only flip `open`. Because render() clears innerHTML but keeps the controller alive, reopening the modal restores the previously typed recipients, role, and message (and a prior success/error), which is almost certainly not the intended fresh-open behavior.

focus-visible ring is only applied to cancel, submit, and recipient-input (lines 300-305). The role <select>, message <textarea>, and pill-remove buttons get no brand focus ring, so keyboard focus indication is inconsistent across the form's controls.

attachListeners() (lines 372-376) binds click handlers to `[part="pill-remove"]`, but at buildStructure time the pills container is empty — no pill-remove buttons exist yet. That binding is dead code; the working handlers are (re)attached in updateDynamic() at lines 426-430.

updateDynamic() rebuilds the entire pills innerHTML and re-binds listeners on every stateChanged emission — including on each keystroke in the message textarea (setMessage -> update -> stateChanged). Recreating DOM on unrelated state changes is wasteful; pills should update only when the recipients array changes.

The `roles` setter calls render(), but when the modal is already open the buildStructure() guard (`querySelector('[part="dialog"]')`) short-circuits, so newly assigned role options are not reflected into the live <select> until the modal is closed and reopened.

summary: A reasonably clean read-only detail panel that uses --boe-token-* variables and safe HTML escaping, but the flagship owner section silently fails on the documented string attribute, dark mode is broken by hardcoded white color-mixes, and interactive states beyond focus-visible are absent.

issues.description: The `owner` getter (line 71-73) runs the attribute through `parseJsonAttribute`, so the documented usage `owner="Morgan Lee"` in docs-site/examples.ts (line 469) makes `JSON.parse("Morgan Lee")` throw, falling back to `null`. The entire owner section (avatar + name) is silently dropped in the primary docs example. The attribute contract is inconsistent: title/eyebrow/status/message are plain strings while owner/meta/actions require JSON, and the docs pass owner as a plain string.

Dark mode is broken: `--_obp-brand-soft` (line 201), owner-avatar background (line 267) and the surface color-mixes hardcode literal `white`/`#fff` (line 333) instead of surface tokens. In dark themes the status badge and avatar render light-on-light. The DS ships box-dark.ts tokens, so these mixes should blend against `--boe-token-surface-surface`, not `white`.

Action buttons implement only `:focus-visible` (line 336). There is no `:hover`, `:active`, or `:disabled` styling, no brand-hover token (SurfaceSurfaceBrandHover/Pressed exist in box-defaults.ts but are unused), and no disabled/loading affordance on actions. Interactive polish expected of a Box button is missing.

The title is a non-semantic `<div part="title">` (line 344) rather than a heading, so it is not exposed in the screen-reader heading outline. The `<section part="panel">` also has no accessible name (no aria-labelledby pointing at the title), leaving the panel an unlabeled generic region.

Overriding the native `title` property/attribute (lines 92-98) shadows HTMLElement.title, causing an unintended native browser tooltip on the host and colliding with the platform semantic. A distinct attribute like `heading`/`item-title` would avoid the clash.

Spacing and radii use arbitrary rem values (padding 1.1rem, gaps 0.55rem/0.82rem, radii 1rem/0.9rem/0.85rem) rather than a consistent Blueprint 4/8px rhythm or radius tokens, so the vertical rhythm and corner treatment are slightly off-grid and inconsistent between the panel, owner card, and meta rows.

Actions declare a `tone` field and style `data-tone="primary"`, but any other tone value (e.g. the docs `tone` omitted defaults to "neutral") gets no differentiated styling, and there is no destructive/danger tone despite tone being a first-class API field.

summary: A token-aware form pattern with clean reflection and events, but undermined by a serious re-render-on-keystroke bug that destroys the focused input, missing hover/active/error/loading states, and a docs example that doesn't actually work against the component's API.

issues.description: Every keystroke re-renders the entire form and loses focus/caret. setFieldValue() (line 142) calls setAttribute("value", …); because "value" is in observedAttributes, attributeChangedCallback fires synchronously and calls this.render() (line 124), which replaces shadowRoot.innerHTML (line 280) and rebuilds every input. Typing one character in a text/textarea field blows away the element the user is typing into, dropping focus and cursor position. The write to valueInternal via the value attribute should not trigger a full re-render during input handling.

The docs example does not work against the component. examples.ts passes title="File properties" but the component observes/reads label, not title (getter at line 71), so it renders the default "Item Form". The example fields also use type: "string" (unsupported; only text|textarea|select|checkbox exist) and per-field value: "Quarterly Plan.pdf"/"Final", but renderField reads values only from valueInternal keyed by field.id (getFieldValue, line 146) and never from field.value — so the seeded values never display. The rendered demo is an empty, mislabeled form.

No hover or active/pressed states anywhere. Inputs, textarea, select, and the submit/cancel buttons have no hover feedback, and the primary submit button never uses the available --boe-token-surface-surface-brand-hover (#0057c0) or -pressed (#004eaa) tokens. Only focus-visible and disabled are implemented.

No error/validation or loading states despite being a form. The <form> is marked novalidate (line 423) and there is no aria-invalid, no error message rendering, no required-field affordance, and no submitting/loading state on the submit button — so a form pattern ships with no way to surface validation or in-flight feedback.

Hardcoded colors bypass tokens. field-value text is color: rgba(35, 32, 28, 0.94) (line 357) instead of --boe-token-text-text (#222222), and the submit button text is color: #fff (line 411) instead of --boe-token-text-text-on-brand. Also border radii are very large for Blueprint (form 1rem, sections 0.9rem, inputs 0.85rem, pill 999px buttons), which reads softer/rounder than Box's tighter radius rhythm.

Field descriptions become part of the accessible name instead of a description. The description <div> is rendered inside the <label> (e.g. lines 205-215, 260-271) alongside the label span, so a screen reader announces label+description concatenated as the control name. It should be a separate element linked via aria-describedby rather than nested in the label.

The form and its sections have no programmatic accessible name. The form title is a plain <div part="label"> (line 424) not linked via aria-labelledby to the <form>, and each <section> uses a <div part="section-label"> (line 430) with no aria-labelledby, so the grouping is visual only. In read mode the field-value (line 199) is likewise not associated with its label span.

The dispatched custom event is named "submit" (line 477), colliding conceptually with the native form submit event; a namespaced name (e.g. item-form-submit) would be clearer and avoid confusion for consumers listening on the host.

summary: A competent SVG line chart with good token usage for brand/text/stroke and clean smooth-path math, but it is undermined by a global-attribute collision on `title`, ARIA role misuse on point buttons, and thin interaction states. Solid mid-tier, not production-grade.

issues.description: The `title` property/attribute (line 38, 109-115) collides with the global HTML `title` attribute. Reflecting to `title` (e.g. title="Active users") also sets the native tooltip, so hovering the host shows a stray browser tooltip. Rename to something namespaced like `heading`/`chart-title` to avoid the collision.

Point buttons are `<button role="listitem">` (lines 266-273). Overriding the implicit button role with role="listitem" strips the button semantics from assistive tech — the interactive control is no longer announced as clickable/pressable. Use a real list wrapper (ul/li or role=list with non-button list items) or drop the listitem role and keep native button semantics.

No hover, active, or focus-visible states on action buttons (lines 373-388) — only point buttons get a focus-visible ring (lines 468-471). Actions have no brand focus ring, no hover feedback, and no :active. There is no disabled, loading, error, or selected state anywhere despite a point-selected event that implies selection.

Accent tone is hardcoded as rgba(90,124,247,0.94) for dots and legend swatches (lines 440, 508) and does NOT match the design token SurfaceStatusSurfaceAccent (#5b4b8a). Success tone rgba(38,194,129) (lines 444, 512) happens to equal #26c281 but is still hardcoded instead of referencing --boe-token-surface-status-surface-success. Primary action uses `color: white` (line 387) rather than --boe-token-text-text-on-brand.

getCoordinates (line 163) seeds the max reduce with 0: `reduce((v,p)=>Math.max(v,p.value), 0)`. For an all-negative dataset the max is wrongly clamped to 0, distorting the y-scale (all points pushed to the same baseline). Seed from points[0].value instead.

render() rewrites shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale on every attributeChangedCallback (line 302) and re-attaches all listeners. Any attribute change (even unrelated ones) destroys DOM and drops keyboard focus from the currently-focused point/action button — poor for interactive re-renders.

The chart svg has role="img" with a generic aria-label of just the title + "trend line" (line 240), conveying no data. Users relying on AT get no access to the actual values; the point-list buttons help but the visualization itself is opaque. Consider a visually-hidden data table or richer description.

Custom events are named "action" and "point-selected" (lines 140, 150) without the design-system's box-/boe- namespace prefix, risking collisions with generic listeners and diverging from the rest of the library's event conventions.

href is only passed through escapeHtml() before being placed in the anchor's href attribute. escapeHtml escapes quotes/angle brackets (preventing attribute breakout) but does NOT block dangerous URL schemes, so href="javascript:alert(1)" is rendered verbatim into <a href> and executes on click. The href should be scheme-validated (allow http/https/mailto/relative, reject javascript:/data:) before rendering.

No disabled state is implemented despite the component being named a 'button' (sibling box-button supports a disabled attribute). There is no way to render a non-interactive/disabled link, nor any loading state. Only hover/active/focus-visible are handled.

aria-label is set to exactly the same string as the visible link text (line 124 vs 126). Duplicating visible text in aria-label is redundant and an accessibility anti-pattern; it adds no information and risks divergence from the visible label. The aria-label should be dropped (visible text already names the link) or reserved for cases where the accessible name must differ.

render() rewrites shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale on every attributeChangedCallback, destroying and recreating the <a>. If any observed attribute (href/label/tone) changes while the link is focused, the anchor is replaced and focus is lost, breaking keyboard interaction. attributeChangedCallback also ignores the name/oldValue/newValue args and re-renders unconditionally even when the value is unchanged.

Default href is '#' (line 22), so a link-button with no href renders as a focusable anchor that navigates to the top of the page / does nothing meaningful. There is also no target/rel support, so external links cannot be marked with rel="noopener noreferrer" for security.

Spacing/radius are hardcoded ad-hoc values rather than Blueprint tokens: border-radius: 0.6rem is an arbitrary non-Blueprint radius, and padding/margin mix em units (0.35em 0.6em) with the rem-based radius. No spacing/radius foundation tokens are used even though the color tokens are correctly wired.

focus-visible uses a soft translucent box-shadow glow (color-mix at 18% alpha) rather than a solid high-contrast brand focus ring. This is a weaker, lower-contrast focus indicator than Box's standard brand ring convention and may not meet the visibility expected of the design system.

No custom events are dispatched (e.g. no click/activate event augmentation), tone is unvalidated (any string is accepted; only 'neutral' and 'danger' have styles while the default is 'primary'), and there is no size attribute. observedAttributes + reflection getters/setters are otherwise correct and consistent with sibling components.

summary: A compact link-styled component that correctly wires --boe-token-* color variables with fallbacks and provides clean attribute reflection, but it is undermined by an unfiltered javascript:-scheme XSS vector in href, no disabled/loading states despite 'button' naming, a redundant aria-label, and focus loss from full innerHTML re-renders. Solid foundation, meaningful gaps for production.

A visually polished menu that uses Blueprint color tokens correctly and has clean attribute/event plumbing, but it fundamentally fails the ARIA menu keyboard-interaction pattern and loses focus on every re-render, so it is not production-quality as an interactive menu.

issues.description: No keyboard navigation is implemented. A role="menu" with role="menuitem" children must support Arrow Up/Down, Home/End and a roving tabindex per the ARIA APG. menu.ts (render, lines 78-166) attaches only 'click' listeners and never handles keydown, so the menu cannot be operated by keyboard as a menu.

Wrong tab semantics for a menu widget: each item is a native <button> (line 81) with no tabindex management, so every item is its own Tab stop. The menu pattern requires a single Tab stop into the menu with arrow keys moving focus between items (roving tabindex). There is no tabindex="-1"/"0" management anywhere.

No focus management: render() never sets initial focus and there is no aria-orientation on the role="menu" container (line 145). Host disabled state is not reflected to the menu container (no aria-disabled on [part=menu]).

render() rewrites this.shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale on every attributeChangedCallback (lines 69-71, 88-148). Any attribute change (e.g. toggling disabled) destroys and recreates all nodes, discarding keyboard focus and interrupting the user. Incremental DOM updates or focus restoration are needed.

The click handler re-reads and re-parses this.items via the getter (line 153, JSON.parse) instead of closing over the item already available at render time (lines 78-86). Each item's JSON is re-parsed on every click, and render() itself parses items twice (map + handlers).

No selected/checked state despite the SurfaceItemSurfaceSelected token existing (used only, oddly, as an :active blend on line 132). Menu items cannot express a selected/checked state (menuitemradio/menuitemcheckbox with aria-checked), so the selected visual token is effectively unused for its purpose.

The drop shadow color is hardcoded (#0b1e33, line 105) rather than derived from a token, and spacing/radius values (0.45rem, 0.75rem, 0.6rem, 0.92rem font-size) are magic numbers not tied to any spacing/radius scale, so shadow and rhythm won't adapt with the design system (e.g. box-dark).

The dispatched event name 'item-selected' (line 159) is un-namespaced, diverging from a boe/box-prefixed convention and risking collisions; the detail also passes the raw item object with no indication of source element.

role="menuitemradio" and aria-checked are hardcoded for every instance (menu-item.ts:124-126). The docs example is label="Rename" — an action, not a selectable radio option — which must be role="menuitem". There is no attribute to select the role, so plain action items are announced incorrectly as unchecked radio items.

Disabled state uses the native <button disabled> attribute (line 128), which removes the item from the focus order. ARIA menu authoring practice requires disabled menu items remain focusable via aria-disabled so roving arrow-key navigation still lands on them. It also redundantly sets both disabled and aria-disabled.

render() rewrites the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every connectedCallback and attributeChangedCallback (line 74). Toggling the selected attribute — exactly what a parent menu does during keyboard navigation — destroys and recreates the <button> node, discarding focus mid-navigation. Attribute changes should patch DOM/attributes, not rebuild it.

The selected background hardcodes white inside color-mix: color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-item-surface-selected) 64%, white 36%) (line 106). In dark mode the selected surface token is #14335a but mixing 36% white washes it toward light, breaking the dark theme. Should mix against a surface token, not literal white.

No active/pressed state and no loading state. More importantly, real Box menu items support a leading icon and trailing shortcut/submenu affordance, but this component exposes only a label attribute with no <slot>, so no icon or rich content can be composed.

Type ramp and radius are magic numbers rather than tokens: font-size: 0.92rem (line 91) and border-radius: 0.6rem (line 87) and padding 0.6rem/0.7rem are hardcoded instead of drawing from spacing/radius/typography scale tokens.

Dispatches a generic event named "selected" (line 140) while selected is also a boolean attribute/property, creating a confusing name collision; the event is also unprefixed (e.g. box-menu-item-select would be clearer).
The label getter defaults to "Menu Item" (line 34), so an item with no label silently renders placeholder text instead of an empty/blank label, which can mask authoring mistakes.

summary: A tidy but under-built menu item that uses correct --boe-token-* variables and has hover/focus-visible/selected/disabled styling, but has real semantic and behavioral defects: a hardcoded menuitemradio role that mis-announces action items, native disabled that breaks menu keyboard nav, a full innerHTML re-render that drops focus on selection changes, and a hardcoded white color-mix that breaks dark mode.

issues.description: Every keystroke in a rule's text input triggers a full shadow DOM rebuild. updateRule() calls setAttribute("rules", ...), and "rules" is in observedAttributes, so attributeChangedCallback fires render() which sets shadowRoot.innerHTML on each input event. This destroys the live <input>, so the field loses focus and caret position after every character — the text input is effectively unusable for typing more than one character. Same destructive re-render happens on select change, add, and remove.

The <select> (field/operator) and <input> (value) controls have no accessible name — no aria-label, no associated <label>, no aria-labelledby. Screen readers announce them as unnamed comboboxes/textboxes. The part="label" element is a plain <div> with no programmatic association to any control (no role="group"/fieldset+legend/aria-labelledby).

No interactive states are implemented. There is no :focus-visible brand focus ring (relies on native outline only), no :hover, no :active/pressed, no disabled, no error, and no loading state — despite tokens existing for them (StrokeStrokeHover, SurfaceSurfaceBrandHover, SurfaceSurfaceBrandPressed, SurfaceStatusSurfaceError). Controls look static across all interaction states.

Focus is lost on remove/add because the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML is regenerated. After clicking a rule's Remove button, focus falls to <body> instead of moving to an adjacent rule or the Add button — no focus management. Multiple identical unnamed "Remove" buttons also give no per-row context (e.g. "Remove rule 2").

Docs example is broken/mismatched: <box-metadata-filter-builder title="Metadata filters"> uses a title attribute that the component never reads (it uses the "label" attribute; observedAttributes = [fields, label, rules]), so the heading falls back to the default "Metadata Filters". The example also passes no fields, so both selects render with zero <option>s and the component demos as empty/non-functional.

Hardcoded brand color instead of a token: the add button border uses `rgba(0, 97, 213, 0.28)` (line 268) rather than a --boe-token-* variable. Several other spots use color-mix against literal `white` (e.g. surface-secondary 94%/white 6%, brand 12%/white 88%), which bypasses tokens and breaks in dark mode.

No dark-mode support and off-grid Blueprint rhythm. Backgrounds are mixed with a literal `white`, which inverts poorly on dark themes. Spacing (0.9rem, 1.1rem, 0.85rem, 0.65rem) and radii (1rem, 0.85rem, 999px) are ad hoc rather than aligned to a consistent Blueprint spacing/radius scale, producing inconsistent rounding and rhythm.

No disabled/read-only attribute support: there is no observed `disabled` attribute and controls cannot be programmatically disabled, so the component can't reflect a non-editable state. rule-removed also dispatches detail.rule which is undefined when index is out of range.

summary: A functional but rough pattern: it uses design tokens in places and dispatches sensible events, but a destructive full-innerHTML re-render on every input makes the text field nearly unusable, form controls lack accessible names, and hover/focus-visible/disabled/error states are entirely absent. The docs example even uses a non-existent `title` attribute with no fields, so it demos empty.
A tokenized, event-emitting inspector with a solid attribute/reflection API, but undermined by invalid definition-list markup, thin interaction-state coverage, and off-grid, partly-hardcoded styling.

issues.description: Invalid DOM/ARIA structure in renderSections(): each <dl part="section-fields"> contains <button> children, and <dt>/<dd> are nested INSIDE those buttons (lines 102-118). A <dl> may only contain <dt>/<dd> (optionally wrapped in <div>), and putting dt/dd inside a button breaks definition-list semantics. Screen readers will not expose these as term/description pairs. Use a valid structure (e.g. div-based rows, or dl with dt/dd and a separate accessible control).

Field buttons dispatch a 'field-selected' event but there is no selected state: no aria-pressed toggling and no visual selected styling (no --boe-token-surface-item-surface-selected #f2f7fd usage). The interaction implies selection yet nothing reflects which field is active. Also missing :active/pressed and any disabled state for the button controls.

No heading semantics or accessible name for the region. The title renders as <div part="title"> (line 259) rather than a heading (h2/h3), and the outer <section part="inspector"> has no aria-labelledby/aria-label, so the landmark/section is unnamed and the title is not in the heading hierarchy.

Off-grid spacing and typography instead of Blueprint rhythm: fractional rem values like 1.1rem padding (line 148), 0.45rem/0.7rem/0.84rem gaps and font-sizes, and letter-spacing 0.06em/0.08em are not on a consistent 4/8px scale, so vertical rhythm and sizing feel ad hoc rather than systematic.

Hardcoded brand color and light-mode-only blending bypass tokens. Line 212 uses rgba(0, 97, 213, 0.22) for the hover border-color instead of a color-mix over --boe-token-surface-surface-brand, and line 151 blends the surface with literal 'white 6%', which will look wrong under the dark token set (box-dark.ts). No dark-mode handling is present.

No transition despite a hover transform. [part="field"]:hover applies transform: translateY(-1px) and a border-color change (lines 211-214) with no 'transition' declared, so the movement snaps abruptly rather than easing — unpolished relative to Box production quality.

Only two tones are styled. field.tone is typed as an open string and defaults to 'neutral', but [part="field-value"] styling only handles data-tone="accent" and "success" (lines 243-249). Common metadata tones like error/warning (tokens SurfaceStatusSurfaceError/Inprogress exist) fall back to the default color with no visual differentiation.

The custom 'title' property/attribute (lines 57-63) shadows the native HTMLElement.title, so the rendered value also becomes a browser tooltip on the host element and collides with the platform semantics. A less overloaded attribute name (e.g. 'heading') would avoid the conflict.

render() replaces the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML and re-binds click listeners on every attributeChangedCallback (lines 137-274). Any single attribute change tears down and rebuilds all field buttons and event listeners, which is wasteful and drops any transient DOM state; there is no diffing or targeted update.

The `trend.tone` field is dead — CSS only styles `[part="trend"]` by `data-direction` (up/down/flat), never by `data-tone`. The docs example passes `trend: { label: "+16.5%", tone: "success" }` with NO direction, so the badge renders as the neutral grey default instead of green. The component's own showcase example is visually wrong. Either honor `data-tone` in CSS or derive direction from tone.

Dark theme is broken. box-dark.ts defines SurfaceSurfaceSecondary=#161616 etc., but the component hardcodes `white` into every color-mix: card bg `white 4%`, status badge `white 90%`, trend up/down `white 86%`. In dark mode these stay light-on-light (e.g. the status pill becomes a light chip on a dark card). Mix against a surface token or use prefers-color-scheme, don't blend toward literal white.

Trend up/down colors are hardcoded hex (#26c281/#138a58 for up, #ed3757/#bf2340 for down) even though semantic tokens exist for exactly this: SurfaceStatusSurfaceSuccess (#26c281) and SurfaceStatusSurfaceError (#ed3757). Should use `var(--boe-token-surface-status-surface-success/error)` so it tracks theme and palette changes.

Trend direction is conveyed by color alone. `data-direction="up"/"down"` only changes background/text color; the label "+16.5%" carries no icon, arrow glyph, or textual/aria indication of rise vs fall. Fails WCAG 1.4.1 (use of color) for color-blind users. Add a directional icon or visually-hidden "increased/decreased" text.

The card has no accessible name and no semantic association between title, value, and trend. `<article>` renders with no aria-label/aria-labelledby, the big value (`1,284`) is a bare div unlinked to its title (`Active shared links`), and the status pill ("Healthy") is purely visual. A screen reader reads a disconnected stream of text. Consider aria-labelledby wiring or a role="group" with an accessible name.

Interaction states are almost entirely missing. The only styled state is `[part="action"]:focus-visible`. The action button has no :hover or :active feedback (brand button should use SurfaceSurfaceBrandHover/Pressed which exist in tokens), and there is no disabled state for the action. No loading/skeleton or error state for the metric value itself.

Using `title` as an observed attribute/property collides with the native global HTMLElement `title`, which renders a browser tooltip on hover and pollutes the accessibility tree with the metric label. The getter also silently defaults to "Metric", masking a missing-title bug. Rename to something like `heading`/`label`.

Only a single generic `action` event is dispatched. The event name is un-namespaced (collides easily), it is not cancelable, and there is no event for other meaningful interactions. Prefer a namespaced, documented event (e.g. `metric-card:action`) and consider marking it cancelable.

Every attribute change calls `render()`, which replaces the entire shadow `innerHTML` and re-attaches the click listener. This throws away focus and DOM state on any update, and rebuilds the full `<style>` block each time. Fine for a static card but wasteful and focus-hostile if attributes change at runtime.

An empty `value` still renders `
` at 1.95rem, leaving a large empty gap, unlike eyebrow/message/status which are conditionally omitted. Guard the value markup or provide a placeholder.

Spacing/type values are off the Blueprint 4/8px grid and use arbitrary rem values (0.95rem padding, 0.76rem, 0.98rem, 0.45rem gap, 0.22rem). Radii are also ad hoc (1rem card, 999px pills) with no radius tokens. Inconsistent rhythm versus a token-driven scale.

summary: A functional but non-production metric card: it uses --boe-token-* variables in places, yet hardcodes white into color-mix (breaking the shipped dark theme), hardcodes status colors instead of the existing success/error tokens, and has a real bug where trend.tone is ignored so its own docs example renders grey instead of green. Accessibility (color-only trend, no accessible name) and interaction states (only focus-visible) are thin.

issues.description: Every checkbox toggle triggers a full re-render that destroys focus. The change handler calls setAttribute('value', ...) (line 213), which fires attributeChangedCallback (line 68) -> render() (line 83), replacing the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML (line 109). The just-toggled input is destroyed and recreated, so keyboard focus is lost after every selection. This makes multi-selecting via keyboard very awkward and is the component's most serious defect.

No disabled state at all. Unlike the sibling checkbox component (which implements :host([disabled]) opacity/cursor and a disabled attribute), multi-select has no 'disabled' in observedAttributes, no disabled styling, and options cannot be individually disabled (MultiSelectOption type is only {label, value}). Users cannot express a non-interactive field or non-selectable option.

No error/invalid state. There is no error attribute, no aria-invalid, no error message slot, and no error-colored styling (e.g. --boe-token-surface-status-surface-error). A form multi-select needs a validation/error presentation and this has none.

Focus indication uses [part="option"]:focus-within (line 181) rather than the brand focus-visible convention used elsewhere ([part="input"]:focus-visible in checkbox.ts line 101). :focus-within fires on mouse click too, so the focus ring appears on pointer interaction, deviating from the keyboard-only brand focus ring pattern.

Redundant double render on programmatic value set. The value setter (lines 58-62) sets valueInternal, calls setAttribute('value', ...) — which itself triggers attributeChangedCallback -> re-parses value and calls render() — and THEN calls render() again directly. Every property assignment parses the value twice and renders twice.

Not form-associated. The component does not use ElementInternals (static formAssociated), exposes no 'name' attribute, and its native checkboxes live in the shadow DOM, so the selected values are never submitted with a surrounding <form>. A forms-directory component named multi-select cannot participate in form submission.

The summary badge ('No selections' / 'N selected', line 201) updates on selection but is a plain <span> with no aria-live region, so screen-reader users get no announcement of the changed selection count. The native checkbox state changes are announced, but the summary is silent.

Hardcoded shadow color not tokenized. The options container box-shadow (line 156) uses a literal '#0b1e33' in color-mix instead of a --boe-token-* variable, so it will not adapt to the dark theme (box-dark.ts) like the rest of the surfaces do.

Selection order is derived from DOM order, not click order. The change handler rebuilds the value array by querying ':checked' in document order (lines 208-210), so the emitted value never reflects the order the user selected items — a subtle correctness gap if consumers care about selection sequence.

summary: A clean, token-driven checkbox-list multi-select with good fieldset/legend semantics and correct attribute reflection, but undermined by a focus-losing full re-render on every toggle and missing disabled/error states and form association.

issues.description: The `transition: inline-size 160ms ease` on [part="sidebar"] never actually animates. `attributeChangedCallback` calls `render()`, which reassigns `shadowRoot.innerHTML` and destroys/recreates the entire shadow tree on every `collapsed` change. The [part="sidebar"] element is a brand-new node each render, so it paints directly at the target width (3.75rem or 15rem) with no starting value to transition from. The declared collapse animation is dead. The fix is to stop re-rendering for state changes: style off `:host([collapsed])` and remove the full innerHTML rebuild so the same persistent node transitions.

The component provides no styling for the slotted nav items (the `<a>` links in the default body slot). There is no `::slotted(a)` rule for hover background, focus-visible ring, active, or selected/current state — despite the design system defining SurfaceItemSurfaceSelected (#f2f7fd) and SurfaceSurfaceHover (#f4f4f4) tokens intended exactly for this. Slotted links fall back to raw browser defaults (underlined blue text), which is far from Box production nav-rail quality and gives no visual affordance for the active route.

The collapsed state is not a real icon-strip treatment. It only narrows the rail to 3.75rem and sets `overflow: hidden`, so text-only nav items (as in the docs example: 'All Files', 'Recents', etc.) are simply clipped mid-word rather than reduced to icons. Collapsing produces a broken-looking rail unless every consumer manually swaps to icon-only content, which the component does nothing to support or document beyond a prose comment.

Spacing and radius values are off Blueprint's 4px grid and arbitrary: padding `0.9rem 0.8rem`, radius `0.95rem`, footer `font-size: 0.86rem`, body gap `0.15rem`, collapsed padding `0.55rem`. Blueprint spacing/type steps are 0.25/0.5/0.75/1rem and standard radii; these fractional values read as hand-tuned magic numbers rather than token-driven rhythm.

Token values are mutated with color-mix rather than used directly, which defeats theming and breaks in dark mode. `background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-secondary) 94%, white 6%)` blends 6% white into the surface — in the dark theme this lightens the panel unexpectedly and diverges from the token. Borders similarly fade the stroke token to 82%/68% opacity. If a theme adjusts the token, the hardcoded mix percentages still fudge the result off-spec.

`overflow: hidden` on [part="sidebar"] clips the focus-visible outline of any focused nav item near the panel edges, and in the collapsed state clips focused links entirely, so keyboard users can lose the visible focus indicator. Combined with the absence of any focus-visible styling for slotted items, keyboard focus tracking through the rail is weak.

The element dispatches no events of its own. Since `collapsed` can be set both externally (by the paired toggle button) and reflected as an attribute, a `collapsedchange` CustomEvent would let ancestors react to state changes; without it the sidebar is write-only and consumers must observe the attribute manually.

`render()` re-serializes and rebuilds the full shadow DOM (including the entire <style> block) on every `label` or `collapsed` change via attributeChangedCallback. This is wasteful and causes style recomputation/flash; the label could be updated by setting `aria-label` on a cached nav node and collapse handled purely via `:host([collapsed])` CSS with no re-render at all.

summary: A deliberately thin structural nav rail: correct `<nav>`/aria-label landmark and clean collapsed/label attribute reflection, but it offloads all interactive state to the consumer and styles nothing it slots. The declared collapse transition is dead because every attribute change rebuilds the whole shadow DOM, and off-grid spacing plus color-mix token mutation hurt fidelity.

A competent, tokenized inline nudge with brand accent, escaped content, and reflected attributes/events, but it forces itself open on connect (ignoring markup state), shadows the native HTMLElement.title property, hardcodes white into color-mix so it won't re-theme in dark mode, and drops focus on dismiss.

issues.description: connectedCallback() unconditionally sets this.openValue = true and renders, ignoring the initial `open` attribute state. Despite `open` being in observedAttributes, a nudge cannot be rendered initially dismissed via markup (e.g. server-rendered closed) — it will always flash open on connect regardless of author intent.

The component overrides the `title` getter/setter and lists `title` in observedAttributes. `title` is a global HTMLElement property/attribute that browsers use for native tooltips; repurposing it as a heading alias collides with that built-in behavior and is a confusing/leaky API choice. `heading` alone should be canonical.

Backgrounds and borders use color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-brand) N%, white) with a hardcoded `white` literal. In a dark theme the tokens change but the mix target stays white, so the tinted surface will not re-theme — producing light chips on a dark surface. Should mix against a surface token, not a literal color.

On dismiss(), render() sets shadowRoot.innerHTML = "" which removes the dismiss button that currently holds focus, so focus is silently dropped to <body>. No focus is moved to a sibling/host or announced, breaking keyboard navigation continuity.

The [part="action"] link-button has focus-visible styling but no :hover (and no :active) state, so pointer users get no affordance feedback on the primary action. Only the dismiss button implements :hover.

role="status" region has no accessible name tied to its heading (no aria-labelledby); the heading is a plain <strong> rather than a heading element, so assistive tech gets the live announcement but no structural/named region relationship.

Spacing, radius, and font sizes are magic rem values (0.65rem gap, 0.7rem/0.8rem padding, 0.8rem/0.2rem/999px radii, 0.88rem/0.85rem font) rather than Blueprint spacing/radius/type tokens, so the component does not track the design system's rhythm scale.

set open sets this.openValue then setAttribute('open'), which fires attributeChangedCallback → render(), after which set open calls render() again — a redundant double render per toggle. Harmless but indicates the reflection/render flow is not guarded.

The brand focus-visible ring is a translucent color-mix(...34%, transparent) outline rather than a solid brand ring; on tinted backgrounds this can read as low-contrast against the WCAG 3:1 non-text contrast requirement for focus indicators.

No error/invalid state exists at all. There is no `error`/`invalid` attribute, no error styling (the SurfaceStatusSurfaceError token #ed3757 is never referenced), no error message slot/region. For a form number-input with min=1/max=100 (as in the docs example), out-of-range input gets zero visual feedback.

The element is not form-associated: no `static formAssociated = true`, no ElementInternals, no hidden input. It will not participate in native `<form>` submission or validation, which is a core expectation for a form control.

No `:active` pressed state and no themed styling for the native number spinner buttons; only hover, focus-visible, and disabled are handled. The spinners render with default UA chrome, breaking the Blueprint look.

min/max are passed through to the native input but the value is never clamped. `set value` normalizes only non-finite to 0 (number-input.ts:96-101) and does not honor min/max, so `el.value = 500` with max=100 is accepted silently. No programmatic clamping or validation.

Clearing the field yields a wrong value: on `input`, `Number(input.value)` is computed (line 209); an empty field gives `Number('') === 0`, which passes the `Number.isFinite` guard and dispatches value 0 instead of representing 'no value'/NaN. A blank field is silently reported as 0.

Label styling deviates from Box Blueprint: uppercase, font-weight 700, letter-spacing 0.08em (lines 147-152). Blueprint field labels are sentence-case, normal/medium weight. The gradient background on the input (lines 161-166) is also non-Box; Blueprint inputs are flat surfaces.

Double render on programmatic set: `set value` calls `setAttribute('value', ...)` then `render()` (lines 99-100), and the resulting attributeChangedCallback (lines 107-113) calls `render()` again. Full innerHTML is rebuilt twice per assignment.

During user typing, `syncValue` updates `valueInternal` and dispatches the event but never reflects back to the `value` attribute (lines 115-124), so `getAttribute('value')` goes stale relative to the `.value` getter. Also only a custom `value-changed` event is dispatched — no standard `input`/`change` events, so consumers expecting DOM-standard events get nothing.

No `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby`, or `aria-errormessage` wiring exists (tied to the missing error state). The label is associated only implicitly by wrapping; there is no id-based `for`/`aria-labelledby` fallback, and no way to expose validation messaging to assistive tech.

Spacing uses off-grid rem values (gap 0.45rem = 7.2px, padding 0.6rem/0.85rem, radius 0.7rem) rather than a consistent 4px Blueprint rhythm, and none reference spacing/radius tokens (hardcoded literals).

summary: A clean, token-driven number-input with correct attribute reflection and a solid hover/focus-visible/disabled baseline, but it stops short of Box production quality: no error state, not form-associated, no min/max clamping, and a blank-field-reads-as-0 bug. Label/gradient styling also drifts from Blueprint.

issues.description: render() fully replaces shadowRoot.innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback. When a keyboard user clicks/activates Next or Previous, emitPageChanged sets the page attribute which triggers a full re-render, destroying the focused <button> node and dropping focus back to <body>. A keyboard user paging through results loses their place on every step and cannot continue tabbing/pressing Enter. Focus should be preserved (e.g. re-focus the same part, or move focus to the opposite button when the active one becomes disabled at the first/last page).

The Previous/Next buttons use a skeuomorphic glossy treatment — a linear-gradient background, an inset white highlight (inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.82)) and a slate drop shadow (0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.04)) — that does not match Box Blueprint's flat button aesthetic. These highlight/shadow values are hardcoded rgba (not --boe-token-* variables) and the white blends assume a light background, so they render incorrectly in the dark theme (box-dark.ts). Blueprint buttons should be flat, token-driven, and theme-aware.

page-size is not guarded against 0. If page-size="0", totalPages = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(total/0)) becomes Infinity, currentPage clamps to page, and the startItem/endItem summary math produces nonsensical values. Attributes that fail to parse (e.g. page="abc") flow through Number() as NaN and cascade into the arithmetic. Inputs should be coerced/clamped to sane finite values.

The displayed page is clamped (currentPage = Math.min(Math.max(1, page), totalPages)) but the reflected page attribute is never normalized. Set page="999" with 9 total pages and the component renders page 9 while the page property still reports 999, so external readers of the attribute get a value that disagrees with what is shown.

border-radius: 0.6rem (~9.6px) is an arbitrary hardcoded value rather than a Blueprint radius token/scale, and the pill-ish radius is inconsistent with Box's smaller (~4-6px) control radii. Spacing values (gap 0.75rem, padding 0.35em/0.8em) are also raw literals rather than spacing tokens.

The component only exposes Previous/Next navigation — there are no numbered page controls and no way to jump to an arbitrary page, and it never surfaces the current page position (only the item range 'Showing X-Y of Z'). For a design-system pagination primitive this is a notably thin API; consider exposing current-page/total-pages display and per-page navigation, or a 'page X of N' label.

Disabled edge buttons use the native disabled attribute, which removes them from the tab order and from assistive-tech discovery entirely; at the first/last page there is no announced explanation of why navigation stopped. Also, on re-render new event listeners are attached to freshly-created buttons each time — functionally fine, but the full teardown/rebuild pattern is what causes the focus loss noted above.

summary: A minimal, token-aware Previous/Next pager with a clean event/attribute API, but let down by a full-innerHTML re-render that destroys keyboard focus on navigation, a non-Blueprint glossy button treatment with hardcoded, non-theme-aware highlight/shadow values, and unguarded edge cases (page-size=0, unclamped page attribute).

A functional native-select matrix with decent event/reflection plumbing, but undermined by an off-brand glossy skeuomorphic control, a broken screen-reader label, a focus-destroying full re-render on every change, and a docs example that renders empty. Not close to Box production quality.

issues.description: Line 299 uses `Access for {name}` but no `.sr-only` rule is ever defined in the shadow-root

The docs example (docs-site/examples.ts:509) is `<box-permission-matrix title="Permissions">`. The component has no `title` attribute — its observed attributes are label/options/subjects/value. So `title` only sets a native tooltip, `label` falls back to "Permission Matrix", and with no subjects/options the table body is empty. The published demo renders an essentially blank card.

updateSubjectRole (line 89) sets `this.value`, which calls setAttribute('value', …) → attributeChangedCallback → full render() that rebuilds shadowRoot.innerHTML on every change. This destroys and recreates every <select>, so keyboard focus is lost after each selection and all event listeners are re-bound each time. State changes should update in place, not re-innerHTML the whole tree.

Selection/value desync: options are only marked `selected` when currentValue[subject.id] === option.value (line 305). When a subject has no stored value the browser auto-selects the first <option>, but this.value never records it — the displayed selection and the model disagree until the user manually changes it, and value-changed never fires for the implicit default.

The select-shell is a glossy skeuomorphic control (linear-gradient fills plus `box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.82), 0 1px 2px rgba(15,23,42,0.04)` on lines 216-218, repeated on focus). This inset-white-highlight/gradient look is off-brand for Box Blueprint's flat surfaces, and the highlight colors are hardcoded RGBA rather than tokens, so they read wrong against box-dark.

Spacing, radius and iconography are arbitrary rather than Blueprint scale/tokens: padding values like 1.1rem/0.85rem/0.72rem, border-radius of 1rem and 0.9rem (very rounded vs Box's restrained radii), and a chevron hand-built from two rotated pseudo-element bars (lines 257-276) instead of an icon token. No spacing/radius tokens are referenced anywhere.

No disabled, readonly, error, or loading states are implemented for the matrix or its rows — there is no way to lock a row, show a validation error on a subject's access, or indicate async loading. Only hover and focus-visible exist; active/pressed is also absent.

The `part="label"` heading (line 279) is a styled <div> not programmatically associated with the table, and the outer <section> has no role/aria-labelledby, so the matrix is not exposed as a named group. There is also no empty-state or caption; with zero subjects the table renders headers over an empty body with no accessible messaging.
escapeHtml (lines 3-10) calls `.replaceAll('>', '>')` twice (lines 7 and 8) — a harmless duplicate that signals unreviewed code.

The focus-visible rule group on lines 238-244 applies border-color and box-shadow to `[part="select"]:focus-visible + [part="select-icon"]` as well as the shell; putting a border/ring on the 0.85rem chevron span is meaningless — only the `:has()` branch does real work, so the first selector is dead styling.

summary: A presentational avatar-plus-metadata card that references --boe-token-* variables but leans on arbitrary spacing, hardcoded white mixes that break in dark mode, and a dead `tone` attribute. It has clean attribute reflection but no ARIA structure, no semantic status coloring, and a NaN sizing bug.

issues.description: The `tone` attribute is read (getter/setter, observedAttributes) and rendered as data-tone on the avatar (line 191), but NO CSS anywhere selects [data-tone]. Tone is entirely dead: passing tone="brand"/"error" changes nothing visually. Either wire per-tone avatar backgrounds/initial colors or remove the attribute.

The `status` badge (lines 181-188) always renders with brand blue (--boe-token-surface-surface-brand) regardless of value, ignoring the semantic status tokens that exist in the design system (SurfaceStatusSurfaceSuccess #26c281, SurfaceStatusSurfaceError #ed3757, SurfaceStatusSurfaceInprogress #f5b31b). status="online"/"error"/"away" all look identical; a persona status should map to those semantic colors.

Not theme-aware: backgrounds mix tokens with literal `white` (color-mix ... white 12-24%, lines 129-150) and the avatar uses `box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.8)` (line 151). In dark mode (box-dark tokens) these white washes and the hardcoded white inset highlight will look wrong/blown-out instead of adapting.

Size has no NaN guard: `Number(this.getAttribute("size") ?? "48")` (line 47) plus `Math.max(32, this.size)` (line 107). A non-numeric attribute like size="large" yields Number(...) = NaN, Math.max(32, NaN) = NaN, producing style="width:NaNpx;height:NaNpx" and a collapsed avatar. Clamp/validate to a numeric fallback.

No semantic grouping or role. The whole card is anonymous <div> soup (part="persona"); there is no role="img"/group or aria-label tying the avatar, name, and status together. When src is present, the img alt is the name (line 109) which then duplicates the visible <strong part="name"> text, causing redundant screen-reader announcement.

The initials fallback <span part="fallback"> (line 110) has no accessible label — screen readers read the raw letters (e.g. "ML"), and when both name and initials are empty it renders a bare "?" with no meaning. The fallback avatar should carry an aria-label (the person's name) or be marked decorative.

No image error/loading handling. If `src` fails to load there is no onerror fallback to the initials — the avatar shows a broken-image/alt state instead of gracefully degrading. There is also no hover/focus affordance, so the component cannot be made interactive/selectable without external CSS.

No events are dispatched. Personas are commonly clickable/selectable (member lists, mention pickers), but the component emits nothing (no select/click custom event) and exposes no selected state, limiting reuse to purely static display.

Convoluted subtitle/description aliasing: subtitle getter falls back to description (line 71) while description getter falls back to the subtitle attribute (line 79), and both are in observedAttributes though render only ever reads `description` (line 111). Two overlapping attributes for one slot is confusing and easy to desync; collapse to one.

Spacing/typography use arbitrary off-grid values rather than Blueprint rhythm: gap 0.85rem (13.6px), padding 0.8rem/0.9rem (12.8/14.4px), radius 1rem, name font-size 0.98rem, description/status 0.8rem. None sit on a clean 4/8px scale, so the card won't align with other Box components.

render() rebuilds the entire shadow DOM via innerHTML on every attributeChangedCallback (line 115). Any attribute change (e.g. updating status) destroys and recreates the , forcing an avatar reload/flicker; there is no diffing or targeted update.

The selected-pill background hardcodes white in the color-mix: `background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-brand,#0061d5) 12%, white 88%)` (line 166). In the box-dark theme the surface is #1c1c1c, so the selected pill renders as a near-white chip on a dark background instead of re-theming. The design system already ships a `SurfaceItemSurfaceSelected` token (#f2f7fd light / #14335a dark) that is the correct token for a selected fill; mixing against a hardcoded literal defeats theming.

render() reassigns the entire shadowRoot.innerHTML on every toggle (lines 100/131/192). Activating a pill via keyboard (Enter/Space) destroys and recreates the focused button, so focus is dropped to the document body after each selection — keyboard users lose their place and cannot rapidly toggle multiple facets. There is no focus restoration after re-render.

The selected text color mixes brand 74% with text token but the base selected background relies on the same broken white mix; combined, in dark mode the contrast of selected pills is unpredictable. Also all spacing, radius, and font sizes are hardcoded literals (gap 0.4rem, padding 0.35rem/0.75rem, font-size 0.82rem, radius 999px); acceptable given no spacing tokens exist, but the magic font sizes (0.82rem/0.86rem) are ad-hoc rather than rhythm-aligned.

Redundant double render: the `value` setter calls setAttribute('value', ...) AND render() (lines 66-68); the setAttribute fires attributeChangedCallback which calls render() again (line 90). toggle() has the same pattern (setAttribute then render, then attributeChangedCallback re-renders). Every value change rebuilds the shadow DOM twice.

attributeChangedCallback ignores oldValue/newValue and always re-parses + renders, even when the attribute value is unchanged, compounding the redundant-render cost. It also does not reconcile valueInternal against the current options, so a selected value that no longer exists in options remains in `value` silently.

No :active / pressed state is defined even though transitions on background/border are present; pressing a pill gives no distinct pressed feedback. Loading and error states are not applicable to a facet cloud, but pressed feedback is a reasonable expectation for a toggle button.

The empty state renders a plain `
No options
` with no role=group/aria-label wrapper, so when options are absent the labelled group and its accessible name disappear entirely rather than presenting an empty labelled region or status.

summary: A competent toggle-pill facet component with solid ARIA (role=group, aria-pressed, native buttons, HTML escaping) and clean token/event API, but held back by a dark-mode-breaking hardcoded white in the selected-pill color-mix and a full-innerHTML re-render on every toggle that drops keyboard focus.

issues.description: No disabled support at all: `disabled` is absent from observedAttributes, there is no host `[disabled]` styling, and the trigger/remove/option buttons are always interactive. Box form fields are expected to have a disabled state; there is also no error or loading state, and no distinct visual 'current' highlight on the keyboard-focused menuitem beyond the browser's :focus-visible ring.

The pill background uses `color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-secondary,#fbfbfb) 70%, white 30%)` — mixing toward literal `white` rather than a token. In the dark theme this forces the pill lighter regardless of the token value, breaking theme fidelity. Similarly the menu uses a hardcoded cool-slate shadow `rgba(15,23,42,0.12)` which conflicts with the system's warm-neutral palette and is not derived from any token.

The `label` attribute is only wired to `aria-label` on the field group and the menu; it is never rendered as a visible label. Other Box form components render a visible label, so this component silently drops the field label from the visual UI (e.g. 'Collaborators' in the docs example is invisible), an inconsistency with the design system.

Tabbing out of the menu with the keyboard does not close it. The outside-close logic is bound only to `pointerdown` (focusout deliberately avoided due to re-render), so keyboard focus can leave the widget while `role="menu"` stays open and `aria-expanded` stays true — an orphaned, inconsistent state for AT users.

The `role="menu"`/`menuitem` pattern is a questionable fit: the popup stays open across multiple selections and re-focuses remaining items (listbox/multiselect behavior), yet items carry no `aria-setsize`/`aria-posinset`, the trigger has `aria-haspopup="menu"`+`aria-expanded` but no `aria-controls` linking to the menu, and add/remove operations fire no live-region announcement so screen-reader users get no feedback when a pill is added or removed.

The public `set value` reflects via `setAttribute('value', ...)` AND then calls `this.render()` directly; since setAttribute triggers attributeChangedCallback which also renders, this double-renders — inconsistent with the internal `commit()` which carefully avoids the double render to preserve focus. Only a coarse `value-changed` event is emitted; there are no granular add/remove events and no `disabled` in observedAttributes.

After adding an option the menu keeps focus but `menuFocusValue`/`pendingFocus` are always reset to `remaining[0]` (the first remaining option), jumping focus to the top of the list rather than the next adjacent option, which is disorienting when adding from the middle. Arrow navigation also clamps rather than wrapping (Home/End exist but Down at the last item and Up at the first are dead), and there is no typeahead.

summary: A thoughtfully built multi-select with strong keyboard/focus management and clean token-with-fallback usage, but it lacks disabled/error/loading states, hides the field label, has a dark-theme-breaking color-mix-to-white and a hardcoded shadow, and leaves the menu open on keyboard tab-out.

issues.description: It is not actually a popover. The surface is rendered inside [part="container"] (position: relative) in normal inline-grid flow, so it pushes sibling content down rather than floating/overlaying anchored to the trigger. A real Box popover needs absolute/fixed positioning with anchoring and a stacking context (z-index). This is the core behavioral gap for the named component.

The panel uses role="dialog" (line 88) but has no accessible name — no aria-label or aria-labelledby. A dialog without a name is a WCAG failure and is announced as an unnamed dialog by screen readers.

No focus management. Opening never moves focus into the panel and closing never restores focus to the trigger. For a role="dialog" this is required; keyboard/AT users are left with focus on the trigger while a dialog is 'open' elsewhere in the DOM.

Escape handling is bound only to the trigger button (line 178). Once a user tabs into the slotted panel content, Escape no longer closes the popover. There is also no click-outside / light-dismiss behavior, which users expect from a popover.

Semantic mismatch: the trigger carries aria-expanded (disclosure pattern, line 168) while the panel is role="dialog" (dialog pattern). These are two different patterns. A dialog trigger should use aria-haspopup="dialog" and aria-controls pointing at the panel id; neither aria-haspopup nor aria-controls is present.

No disabled support at all — 'disabled' is not in observedAttributes (line 13), the button is never given the disabled attribute, and there is no disabled styling. There is also no :active state on the trigger; only :hover and :focus-visible are styled.

Double render on toggle. The open setter calls setAttribute('open','') which triggers attributeChangedCallback → render(), then the setter itself calls render() again (lines 36-42). Every toggle rebuilds shadow innerHTML twice and re-binds listeners twice within the same operation.

Spacing, radius, and min-height use arbitrary non-grid values (0.4rem/0.78rem padding, 0.85rem and 1.1rem radii, 0.95rem panel padding) rather than Blueprint 4px/8px rhythm and a token-based radius scale.

Shadows and highlight overlays are hardcoded rgba values (rgba(15,23,42,...), inset rgba(255,255,255,0.74/0.8) on lines 126-127, 162-163) instead of --boe-token-* elevation tokens, and the multi-stop gradients with white mixes read as skeuomorphic rather than Box's flatter, cool-neutral Blueprint surfaces.
The focus-visible ring is a translucent color-mix of brand at 34% alpha (line 145) rather than a solid brand focus ring; Box Blueprint expects a solid, high-contrast brand focus indicator.

Only a single 'open-changed' event is dispatched (line 41). There are no dedicated open/close (or before-toggle/cancelable) events, and the event is not fired when the open attribute is toggled externally (attributeChangedCallback path, lines 57-63, updates state and re-renders but emits nothing).

summary: A component named "popover" that is really an inline disclosure widget: it renders in document flow instead of floating/anchored, and its role="dialog" lacks an accessible name, focus management, robust Escape, and light-dismiss. It does use --boe-token-* variables and reflects label/open, but spacing/radii are off-grid, shadows are hardcoded, and there is no disabled/active state.

A well-constructed, XSS-safe live-presence avatar pile with solid attribute reflection and a polite live region, but it dispatches no DOM events, surfaces no connection/loading/error states despite being network-backed, and mixes tokens with hardcoded white that breaks in dark mode.

issues.description: Avatar and overflow backgrounds mix a token with a literal `white` (e.g. `color-mix(in srgb, var(--boe-token-surface-surface-brand, #0061d5) 12%, white 88%)` on [part="avatar"] and `... surface-surface-secondary 82%, white 18%` on [part="overflow"]). In dark mode SurfaceSurface is #1c1c1c, so these chips stay light against dark surfaces. Mix against a surface token instead of the `white` literal so the pile respects the active theme.

The element dispatches no DOM CustomEvents. The underlying PresenceController emits `presenceChanged`, `connected`, and `disconnected`, but BoxPresenceElement only re-renders on `presenceChanged` and never re-emits anything on itself, so a host page cannot listen (e.g. `addEventListener('presencechanged', ...)`) for roster or connection changes. Re-dispatch these as CustomEvents from the element.

No connection, loading, or error state is rendered. The controller tracks `connected` and emits `connected`/`disconnected`, but the element ignores them — while the transport is connecting the widget shows only 'No one else here', and a dropped feed looks identical to an empty roster. A live network-backed presence surface should indicate connecting/offline.

Setting the `users` property while a transport-driven controller is active silently has no visible effect: the setter stores to `usersValue` but skips `render()` when a controller exists, and the getter returns `controller.users`. The dropped assignment is easy to trip over; either warn, ignore explicitly by contract, or re-render.
The overflow indicator (`+3`) has no aria-label, so assistive tech announces the bare '+3' rather than something like '3 more people'. Add a descriptive label to the overflow node.

Spacing, avatar size, and typography use arbitrary rem literals (gap 0.6rem, margin-inline-start -0.5rem, size 2rem, font-size 0.76rem/0.84rem) rather than Blueprint spacing/size tokens, so the pile is not tied to the design-system rhythm scale.

The polite aria-live summary re-announces the full roster summary on every transport update; with a frequently-changing feed this can produce chatty, queued announcements. Consider debouncing or only announcing meaningful membership deltas.

Action buttons ([part="action"], lines 371-387) define only a base and a data-tone="primary" style. There is NO :hover, :focus-visible, or :active styling anywhere. Keyboard users get no brand focus ring (Blueprint calls for a visible focus indicator), and pointer users get no hover affordance. For a component whose only interactive elements are these buttons, this is a core states gap.

No disabled/loading/error states exist. PreviewElementAction has only {id,label,tone} — no `disabled`, so actions can never be non-interactive. The 'status' text can say 'Connecting'/'Ready' (line 240) but there is no loading skeleton/spinner on the stage and no error tone/styling for [part="message"]; an error message renders identically to an informational one.

The title renders as <div part="title"> (line 444), not a heading element. The widget exposes no document outline / landmark heading. Combined with the absence of a host role, assistive tech sees an unlabeled generic <article> group with a styled div, not a titled preview region.

render() replaces this.shadowRoot.innerHTML wholesale on every attributeChangedCallback AND on every providerAdapter.subscribe() callback (lines 140-144, 274). Any adapter state push (e.g. page/zoom change) destroys and recreates all buttons, so keyboard focus on a focused action button is silently lost, and click listeners are re-bound on every render. This is a real focus-management/perf bug for a live-updating preview surface.

Status changes are not announced. providerStatus / adapterState.ready toggle between 'Ready' and 'Connecting' with no aria-live region, so screen-reader users get no notification when the preview finishes loading or the provider status changes.

Overriding HTMLElement's native `title` property (getter/setter, lines 124-130) collides with the built-in `title` attribute, which the browser renders as a native tooltip. Setting the component title also produces a stray hover tooltip, and the default 'Preview Element' value forces a title attribute onto the host. A non-colliding name (e.g. `heading`/`label`) would be safer.

Dark-theme-hostile color-mix: --_obp-brand-soft mixes brand with literal `white 88%` (line 286), and [part="stage"] gradients mix with `white 14%` (lines 405-406). These hardcode white rather than a surface token, so under a dark design system (box-dark.ts exists) the provider/status pills and stage stay light — they won't track the theme.

Primary action text color is hardcoded `color: #fff` (line 386) even though a TextTextOnBrand token (#ffffff) exists in box-defaults. On-brand text should use var(--boe-token-text-text-on-brand) so it stays correct if the brand-on-text contrast pairing changes per theme.

Spacing values are off any consistent scale (0.18rem, 0.45rem, 0.35rem, 0.72rem, 1.35rem font, letter-spacing 0.06em). Blueprint rhythm is typically a 4px/8px grid; these ad-hoc rem values are hardcoded rather than drawn from spacing/radius tokens, undermining cross-component consistency.

The [part="stage"] preview surface has no empty/placeholder state. When no slotted [slot="stage"] content is provided it renders an empty gradient box (min-block-size:19rem) with no message, giving no signal that a provider needs to mount content.

The [part="actions"] toolbar group has no accessible group label/role, unlike [part="adapter-meta"] and [part="capabilities"] which set aria-label (lines 228, 248). A set of action buttons with no grouping label is less discoverable.

summary: A well-structured, token-aware layout scaffold with clean attribute reflection and meaningful events, but it falls short of Box production quality: interactive states (hover/focus-visible/active/disabled/loading/error) are essentially absent, the title isn't a heading, status changes aren't announced, and full-innerHTML re-renders drop keyboard focus on live updates.

issues.description: The title is rendered as a plain <div part="title"> (font-size:1.6rem, weight 700) rather than a real heading element (h1/h2). A preview header's document title has no heading semantics, so screen-reader users get no landmark/heading to navigate to, and the <section part="header"> has no aria-label/aria-labelledby, leaving the region unnamed.

The component overrides the native HTMLElement.title property (get/set at lines 64-70). Setting `title` therefore reflects to the global `title` attribute, which triggers a native browser tooltip on hover over the entire host element, and shadows the standard DOM API. This is a real footgun; the display title should use a differently-named attribute (e.g. `heading`) to avoid colliding with native semantics.

The docs example (examples.ts line 491) passes `eyebrow="PDF · 2.4 MB"`, but the component has no `eyebrow` support: it is absent from observedAttributes (line 24), has no getter/setter, and is never rendered. The documented example silently drops a prop, and sibling components (item-details-panel) do support `eyebrow`, so this is an inconsistency/gap.

No hover or active/pressed states are defined for [part="action"] or [part="breadcrumb"]. Only focus-visible is styled (lines 258-262).
